Validated.Unnamed: 2: HR+, HER2-low (IHC 1+ or 2+ without amplification) or HER2-negative (IHC 0)
Validated.Unnamed: 3: ESR1 mutation (ctDNA)
Validated.Unnamed: 5: TNBC; PD-L1-negative (CPS <10) or PD-L1-positive with prior PD-(L)1 inhibitor in curative setting
Validated.Unnamed: 7: KRAS G12X mutation (primary); broader RAS mutations also enrolled
Validated.Unnamed: 13: NPM1 mutation or KMT2A rearrangement
Validated.Unnamed: 14: HER2 activating mutations in the tyrosine kinase domain (TKD)
Validated.Unnamed: 15: EGFR wild-type and ALK wild-type (no actionable genomic alterations)
Validated.Unnamed: 16: Prior progression on PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors; no specific PD-L1 expression or mutation status required for eligibility
Validated.Unnamed: 17: None (pragmatic design with broad eligibility)
Validated.Unnamed: 18: HER2-low (IHC 1+ or IHC 2+/ISH-) or HER2-ultralow (IHC 0 with membrane staining), HR+ (estrogen receptor and/or progesterone receptor positive)
Validated.Unnamed: 20: PIK3CA mutation (present in up to 40% of HR+/HER2- breast cancer)
Validated.Unnamed: 21: HER2-positive (defined as HER2 3+ by immunohistochemistry or HER2 2+ with amplification by in situ hybridization per ASCO-CAP guidelines)
Validated.Unnamed: 22: BRAF V600E mutation
Validated.Unnamed: 24: Somatic alterations in PI3K pathway genes, specifically PIK3CA (exon 9 or 20 hotspot mutations), PIK3R1, or PTEN
Validated.Unnamed: 28: Deleterious germline or somatic HRR gene mutations (BRCA1, BRCA2, BRIP1, CDK12, CHEK2, FANCA, PALB2, RAD51B, RAD54L); ~25% of mCSPC patients
Validated.Unnamed: 30: PSMA PET/CT positivity; PSA doubling time <12 months; PSA ≥0.2 ng/mL (post-RP) or ≥2 ng/mL above nadir (post-RT)
Validated.Unnamed: 34: HR+, HER2-, PIK3CA mutation
Validated.Unnamed: 36: SSTR-positive status (confirmed by SSTR-PET imaging, e.g., Ga-68-DOTATATE PET scan)
Validated.Unnamed: 38: Not specified (DLL3 expression assessed retrospectively; 95% of evaluable patients were DLL3-positive)
Validated.Unnamed: 39: ER+ (defined as ≥10% ER positivity by immunohistochemistry), HER2- (defined as a score of 0, 1+ by immunohistochemistry or a negative in situ hybridization based on single-probe average HER2 copy number)
Validated.Unnamed: 40: ER+, HER2-
Validated.Unnamed: 41: No specific biomarker requirements for eligibility; only histologic confirmation of well- or moderately-differentiated neuroendocrine tumor is required.
Validated.Unnamed: 42: PIK3CA gene mutations, HR+, HER2-
Validated.Unnamed: 44: No specific biomarker requirements
Validated.Unnamed: 45: ER+, HER2-
Validated.Unnamed: 47: H3K27M mutation confirmed by immunohistochemistry or next-generation sequencing
Validated.Unnamed: 48: CD20+ (cluster of differentiation 20 positive)
Validated.Unnamed: 49: Germline BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutation
Validated.Unnamed: 50: dMMR/MSI-H
Validated.Unnamed: 51: MSI-H/dMMR
Validated.Unnamed: 52: BRAF V600 mutation status assessment required for eligibility; patients with BRAFV600 mutation-positive melanoma are eligible if treatment naive and without rapidly progressive disease.
Validated.Unnamed: 53: Triple negative (ER < 10%, PR < 10%, HER2-negative per ASCO/CAP guidelines); no specific PD-L1 or other biomarker requirement for eligibility in most neoadjuvant trials, though PD-L1 status may be assessed for exploratory purposes.
Validated.Unnamed: 54: MSI-H/dMMR
Validated.Unnamed: 56: EGFR exon 19del or L858R mutations
Validated.Unnamed: 58: Triple negative (ER <1%, PR <1%, HER2-negative per ASCO/CAP guidelines)
Validated.Unnamed: 59: Histologically confirmed mantle cell lymphoma with cyclin D1 expression and/or t(11;14) translocation
Validated.Unnamed: 60: HLA-A2+ status, MGMT methylation status assessment
Validated.Unnamed: 61: ER+ (positive for ER and/or PgR), HER2-negative
Validated.Unnamed: 62: Germline BRCA1/2 mutations, HER2-negative
Validated.Unnamed: 63: KRAS G12C mutation
Validated.Unnamed: 64: EGFR mutations (G719X, exon 19 deletion, L858R, or L861Q)
Validated.Unnamed: 65: Mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) or microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H)
Validated.Unnamed: 66: No specific biomarker requirements for eligibility; tumor tissue samples required for B7-H3 expression analysis
Validated.Unnamed: 69: EGFR exon 19 deletion or exon 21 L858R substitution
Validated.Unnamed: 70: HER2-positive (defined as HER2 3+ immunohistochemical overexpression or HER2 amplification by ASCO-CAP guidelines)
Validated.Unnamed: 71: EGFR and ALK wild-type (no actionable genomic alterations)
Validated.Unnamed: 72: PD-L1 tumor cell (TC) expression ≥ 1%; absence of actionable driver oncogene mutations (e.g., EGFR, ALK, ROS1) for which there are locally approved targeted first-line therapies
Validated.Unnamed: 73: EGFR mutations (specifically somatic activating sensitizing mutations such as exon 19 deletions and exon 21 L858R; non-sensitizing mutations like exon 20 insertions are excluded)
Validated.Unnamed: 74: ALK rearrangement (fusion) detected by an FDA-approved test
Validated.Unnamed: 75: Triple-negative status (ER-negative, PR-negative, HER2-negative); Cohort 1b specifically requires BRCA1, BRCA2, or PALB2 mutations
Validated.Unnamed: 76: Hormone receptor-positive (ER and/or PR ≥10% staining), HER2-negative

Validated.Unnamed: 2: TROP2-directed antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) delivering a topoisomerase I inhibitor (DXd) payload via a tetrapeptide-based cleavable linker, resulting in selective tumor cell death and a bystander antitumor effect due to membrane-permeable payload release
Validated.Unnamed: 3: Oral selective estrogen receptor degrader (SERD) that binds to and degrades the estrogen receptor, overcoming resistance mechanisms such as ESR1 mutations
Validated.Unnamed: 4: Oral selective estrogen receptor degrader (SERD) that binds to and degrades the estrogen receptor, thereby inhibiting estrogen-dependent signaling in breast cancer cells
Validated.Unnamed: 5: Antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) targeting TROP2 with SN-38 topoisomerase I inhibitor payload via hydrolysable linker; enables intracellular and extracellular (bystander) cytotoxic effects
Validated.Unnamed: 6: Antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) targeting TROP2 with topoisomerase I inhibitor payload, combined with PD-1 checkpoint blockade
Validated.Unnamed: 7: Oral tri-complex inhibitor that binds cyclophilin A and active GTP-bound RAS(ON), blocking RAS-effector interactions
Validated.Unnamed: 8: Oral RAS(ON) inhibitor that hijacks cyclophilin A (CypA) to form a tripartite complex with active, GTP-bound RAS proteins, blocking downstream signaling and inducing tumor regression
Validated.Unnamed: 10: PD-1/TIGIT bispecific antibody with VEGF blockade
Validated.Unnamed: 11: Anti-PD-1/TIGIT bispecific monoclonal antibody that blocks PD-1 and TIGIT proteins, helping the immune system find and remove tumor cells
Validated.Unnamed: 13: Menin inhibitor that disrupts the menin-KMT2A interaction, leading to downregulation of HOX and MEIS1 genes
Validated.Unnamed: 14: Irreversible tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI) targeting HER2, with activity against EGFR and HER4
Validated.Unnamed: 15: Ceralasertib: selective ATR kinase inhibitor inducing DNA damage and type I interferon-mediated immunomodulation; Durvalumab: anti-PD-L1 monoclonal antibody blocking PD-L1/PD-1 interaction to enhance T-cell activation
Validated.Unnamed: 16: Anti-CTLA-4 monoclonal antibody that blocks the inhibitory signal resulting from CTLA-4 binding to CD80/86, leading to prolongation and enhancement of T-cell activation and expansion. This results in direct activation of CD4+ and CD8+ effector T cells, expansion of tumor antigen-specific cytotoxic T cells, and reduction of regulatory T cell (Treg) suppressive activity.
Validated.Unnamed: 17: Ramucirumab: monoclonal antibody targeting VEGFR2, blocking angiogenesis. Pembrolizumab: anti-PD-1 antibody releasing T-cell inhibition. Combination modulates tumor microenvironment through dual anti-angiogenic and immune checkpoint blockade.
Validated.Unnamed: 18: HER2-targeted antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) with topoisomerase I inhibitor payload
Validated.Unnamed: 19: HER2-targeted antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) with a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (pamirtecan, a derivative of exatecan) linked via a cleavable linker, designed for selective delivery and release of cytotoxic agent in HER2-positive tumor cells, with potential for bystander effect and immune activation
Validated.Unnamed: 20: Inavolisib: selective PI3Kα inhibitor that inhibits and degrades mutant p110α, blocking PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway; Palbociclib: CDK4/6 inhibitor blocking cell cycle progression; Fulvestrant: selective estrogen receptor degrader (SERD)
Validated.Unnamed: 21: Site-specific, construct-homogeneous antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) with drug-to-antibody ratio (DAR) of ~2, using EuCODE technology. Anti-HER2 monoclonal antibody conjugated to Amberstatin 269 (AS269), a potent tubulin inhibitor payload.
Validated.Unnamed: 22: Encorafenib is a selective BRAF V600E inhibitor blocking MAPK signaling; cetuximab is an anti-EGFR antibody preventing MAPK pathway reactivation; mFOLFOX6 chemotherapy targets rapidly dividing cells
Validated.Unnamed: 23: Atezolizumab is a humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody that binds to PD-L1, blocking its interaction with PD-1 and CD80, thereby reactivating T cells to enhance antitumor immunity. Combined with mFOLFOX6 chemotherapy (oxaliplatin, 5-fluorouracil, leucovorin).
Validated.Unnamed: 24: COX inhibitor that suppresses PI3K/Akt signaling pathway activation, reducing cancer cell survival and proliferation
Validated.Unnamed: 25: Autologous tumor infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) therapy + anti-PD-1 antibody
Validated.Unnamed: 26: Fianlimab is a high-affinity, fully human, hinge-stabilized IgG4 monoclonal antibody that blocks LAG-3/MHC class II-driven T-cell inhibition. Cemiplimab is an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks the interactions of PD-1 with PD-L1 and PD-L2, thereby preventing cancer cells from suppressing T-cell activation. The combination of fianlimab and cemiplimab provides dual immune checkpoint blockade, enhancing antitumor immune responses by simultaneously inhibiting both LAG-3 and PD-1 pathways.
Validated.Unnamed: 27: Therapeutic cancer vaccine (T-win platform) targeting IDO1 and PD-L1 pathways, designed to activate and expand T cells against IDO1+ and PD-L1+ tumor and immune-suppressive cells in the tumor microenvironment, thereby modulating the TME to potentiate anti-tumor activity and synergize with anti-PD-1 therapy
Validated.Unnamed: 28: PARP inhibitor (niraparib blocks PARP1/2, impairing DNA repair in HRR-deficient tumors) + CYP17 inhibitor (abiraterone acetate reduces androgen biosynthesis) + corticosteroid (prednisone)
Validated.Unnamed: 29: Oncolytic virus immunotherapy (intraprostatic) + prodrug (valacyclovir): The oncolytic virus (CAN-2409) is injected directly into the prostate, where it selectively infects and replicates in tumor cells, causing direct oncolysis. The virus is engineered to express herpes simplex virus thymidine kinase (HSV-tk), which converts the prodrug valacyclovir into its active form, leading to further tumor cell death. This process releases tumor-associated antigens and danger signals, stimulating both local and systemic anti-tumor immune responses. The combination with radiation further enhances tumor cell killing and immune activation.
Validated.Unnamed: 30: Androgen receptor inhibitor that binds the androgen receptor ligand-binding domain, preventing nuclear translocation and blocking androgen-responsive gene expression
Validated.Unnamed: 31: Lurbinectedin binds to DNA in the minor groove, preferentially at CG-rich sequences, causing irreversible stalling and degradation of RNA polymerase II, leading to transcription inhibition, DNA breaks, and apoptosis. Atezolizumab is a monoclonal antibody that binds to PD-L1 on tumor and immune cells, blocking its interaction with PD-1 and CD80, thereby reactivating T-cell antitumor activity.
Validated.Unnamed: 33: Antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) targeting HER2 with a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload, designed to selectively deliver cytotoxic drug to tumor cells expressing HER2 antigen. The antibody binds to HER2 on the cell surface, the ADC is internalized, and the linker is cleaved by lysosomal proteases, releasing the topoisomerase I inhibitor which causes DNA damage and cell death.
Validated.Unnamed: 34: Selective PI3Kα inhibitor that blocks the PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling pathway and promotes degradation of mutated p110α protein
Validated.Unnamed: 35: CD20×CD3 bispecific antibody that binds CD20 on B-cells and CD3 on T-cells, leading to T-cell activation and B-cell lysis
Validated.Unnamed: 36: Targeted radiopharmaceutical therapy - radiolabeled somatostatin analog binds to somatostatin receptors (primarily sst2) on tumor cells, is internalized, and delivers beta radiation that causes DNA damage (primarily double-strand breaks), leading to tumor cell death.
Validated.Unnamed: 37: Enfortumab vedotin, a Nectin-4 antibody-drug conjugate, binds to Nectin-4 on tumor cells, is internalized via receptor-mediated endocytosis, and releases the microtubule-disrupting agent monomethyl auristatin E (MMAE), which causes cell-cycle arrest and apoptosis. Pembrolizumab, a PD-1 inhibitor, blocks PD-1 on T cells, preventing its interaction with PD-L1/PD-L2, thereby enhancing anti-tumor immune response.
Validated.Unnamed: 38: Bispecific T-cell engager targeting DLL3 and CD3, which binds DLL3 on tumor cells and CD3 on T cells, bringing them into close proximity to form an immunological synapse, resulting in T-cell activation, release of inflammatory cytokines (such as perforin and granzyme B), and lysis of DLL3-expressing tumor cells.
Validated.Unnamed: 39: Oral selective estrogen receptor degrader (SERD) that binds to the estrogen receptor, induces an inactive conformation, and promotes proteasome-mediated degradation of the receptor protein, thereby inhibiting estrogen-dependent and independent ER signaling.
Validated.Unnamed: 40: Oral selective estrogen receptor degrader (SERD) that binds to the estrogen receptor, induces an inactive conformation, and promotes proteasome-mediated degradation of the receptor protein, thereby preventing estrogen-driven transcription and cancer growth.
Validated.Unnamed: 41: Oral tyrosine kinase inhibitor that inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases including VEGFR2, MET, AXL, and RET, thereby blocking downstream signaling pathways involved in tumor cell proliferation, angiogenesis, migration, invasion, and drug resistance.
Validated.Unnamed: 42: Inavolisib is a highly selective PI3Kα inhibitor that binds to the ATP binding site of the p110α subunit, inhibiting its catalytic activity and preventing the phosphorylation of PIP2 to PIP3. This results in decreased activation of the AKT/mTOR pathway, reducing downstream signaling involved in cell growth, survival, and metabolism. Inavolisib uniquely induces the degradation of the mutant p110α protein, providing sustained suppression of the oncogenic PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway.
Validated.Unnamed: 43: BCMA×CD3 bispecific antibody that bridges BCMA on myeloma cells with CD3 on T cells to induce T-cell activation and targeted lysis of myeloma cells
Validated.Unnamed: 44: Non-covalent (reversible) Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitor that binds to BTK and inhibits its activity, blocking B-cell receptor signaling and downstream survival pathways in malignant B-cells
Validated.Unnamed: 45: Oral selective estrogen receptor degrader (SERD) that binds to and promotes degradation of the estrogen receptor, combined with an mTOR inhibitor that blocks the mTOR signaling pathway, thereby inhibiting cell proliferation and survival in cancer cells
Validated.Unnamed: 46: Combined inhibition of BRAF and EGFR blocks the MAPK signaling pathway at multiple points, preventing adaptive feedback reactivation and maintaining suppression of cell proliferation signals in BRAF V600E-mutant colorectal cancer.
Validated.Unnamed: 47: ONC201 is a dopamine receptor D2 (DRD2) antagonist and mitochondrial ClpP protease agonist. It induces the integrated stress response (ISR) and tumor necrosis factor-related apoptosis-inducing ligand (TRAIL) pathways, leading to upregulation of death receptor 5 (DR5), inhibition of the AKT/mTOR/S6 pathways, and activation of apoptosis and cell cycle arrest. Its cytotoxic effects are mediated through both DRD2 antagonism and ClpP activation, with ClpP activation being essential for its antitumor activity.
Validated.Unnamed: 48: CD20×CD3 bispecific antibody that binds CD20 on B-cells and CD3 on T-cells, leading to T-cell activation and B-cell lysis
Validated.Unnamed: 49: PARP inhibitor that blocks PARP catalytic activity and traps PARP on DNA, preventing DNA repair and causing synthetic lethality in BRCA-deficient cells
Validated.Unnamed: 50: PD-L1 inhibitor (immune checkpoint inhibitor) that blocks PD-L1/PD-1 interaction to restore T cell-mediated anti-tumor immune responses
Validated.Unnamed: 51: Dual immune checkpoint blockade: nivolumab (PD-1 inhibitor) and ipilimumab (CTLA-4 inhibitor) work synergistically to release T cell inhibition and enhance antitumor immune responses
Validated.Unnamed: 52: Dual checkpoint inhibition targeting both LAG-3 and PD-1: fianlimab blocks LAG-3, preventing its interaction with MHC class II and other ligands, while cemiplimab blocks PD-1, preventing its interaction with PD-L1 and PD-L2. This dual blockade reactivates exhausted T cells and enhances anti-tumor immune responses by overcoming complementary inhibitory pathways.
Validated.Unnamed: 53: Pembrolizumab is a monoclonal antibody that binds with high affinity to the PD-1 receptor on T cells, blocking its interaction with the ligands PD-L1 and PD-L2. This blockade prevents the downregulation of T cell activation and restores the immune response against tumor cells, leading to enhanced antitumor activity.
Validated.Unnamed: 54: PD-1 inhibitor: blocks the interaction between PD-1 on T cells and PD-L1 on tumor cells, preventing immune suppression and restoring T cell-mediated anti-tumor activity
Validated.Unnamed: 55: ROR1-targeting antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) that binds to ROR1 on tumor cells, is internalized, and releases a cytotoxic payload (MMAE) to induce tumor cell death
Validated.Unnamed: 56: Amivantamab: Bispecific antibody targeting EGFR and MET with immune cell-directing activity (including ADCC, ADCP, and trogocytosis), ligand blocking, and receptor internalization/degradation; Lazertinib: Third-generation, irreversible EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI) that selectively inhibits EGFR activating and resistance mutations (including Ex19del, L858R, T790M) by forming a covalent bond to Cys797 in the ATP-binding site, sparing wild-type EGFR.
Validated.Unnamed: 57: PD-L1 inhibitor that blocks the interaction between PD-L1 on tumor cells and PD-1 on T cells, thereby preventing immune suppression and restoring T cell-mediated anti-tumor immunity
Validated.Unnamed: 58: Cancer vaccine that stimulates the immune system to recognize alpha-lactalbumin antigen, leading to antigen presentation by dendritic cells and activation of CD4+ and CD8+ T cells for anti-tumor immune response, including production of antigen-specific IgG antibodies and IFN-γ-secreting T cells.
Validated.Unnamed: 59: BCL2 inhibitor that selectively binds to BCL-2, displacing pro-apoptotic proteins and triggering apoptosis in cancer cells
Validated.Unnamed: 60: Dendritic cell vaccine with synthetic peptides targeting tumor-associated antigens, designed to activate dendritic cells and induce antigen-specific T-cell responses against tumor cells
Validated.Unnamed: 61: Selective estrogen receptor degrader (SERD) that binds to the estrogen receptor, induces a conformational change, and promotes its degradation via the ubiquitin-proteasome system, thereby inhibiting estrogen signaling and tumor growth.
Validated.Unnamed: 62: PARP inhibitor that blocks DNA repair and traps PARP proteins on DNA, causing synthetic lethality in cells with homologous recombination deficiency (e.g., BRCA1/2 mutations)
Validated.Unnamed: 63: KRAS G12C inhibitors covalently bind to the mutated cysteine residue at position 12 of KRAS, trapping the protein in an inactive GDP-bound state and preventing nucleotide exchange to the active GTP-bound form, thereby inhibiting downstream oncogenic signaling.
Validated.Unnamed: 64: TROP2-targeted antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) with a novel pyrimidine-thiol linker conjugated to a belotecan-derivative topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (DAR 7.4); the hydrolytically sensitive linker permits both extracellular pH-sensitive cleavage and intracellular enzymatic cleavage to release the membrane-permeable payload, enabling a bystander effect
Validated.Unnamed: 65: PD-1 inhibitor (monoclonal antibody that blocks PD-1/PD-L1 interaction, restoring T cell-mediated anti-tumor immune responses)
Validated.Unnamed: 66: B7-H3-directed antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) comprising a humanized anti-B7-H3 IgG1 monoclonal antibody attached to topoisomerase I inhibitor payloads (exatecan derivative, DXd) via tetrapeptide-based cleavable linkers, designed to enhance selective tumor cell death and reduce systemic exposure
Validated.Unnamed: 67: Antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) targeting TROP2 with a topoisomerase I inhibitor (SN-38) payload. The anti-TROP2 antibody binds to TROP2-expressing tumor cells, leading to internalization and lysosomal degradation, which releases SN-38 to inhibit topoisomerase I and induce DNA damage and apoptosis in tumor cells.
Validated.Unnamed: 68: Oral, reversible tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI) that potently inhibits mutant HER2 (including HER2 exon 20 insertions and HER2 point mutations) and EGFR, with high selectivity for mutant vs wild-type EGFR
Validated.Unnamed: 69: Bispecific antibody-drug conjugate targeting EGFR and HER3, binding to both receptors on tumor cells, internalizing via endocytosis, and releasing a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload to induce tumor cell death.
Validated.Unnamed: 70: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) is a HER2-directed antibody-drug conjugate comprising trastuzumab linked to a topoisomerase I inhibitor (deruxtecan) via a cleavable linker. The antibody binds HER2 on tumor cells, leading to internalization and lysosomal degradation, which releases the cytotoxic payload to induce DNA damage and cell death. Additionally, T-DXd can release its payload extracellularly in the tumor microenvironment, independent of HER2 engagement, via cleavage by cathepsin L. The drug also exhibits immune-boosting effects, including antibody-dependent cellular phagocytosis (ADCP) and activation of immunogenic cell death pathways, which further stimulate myeloid cells and enhance tumor antigen presentation to CD8+ T cells.
Validated.Unnamed: 71: ATR kinase inhibitor (ceralasertib) disrupts DNA damage response and activates innate immune pathways (cGAS-STING), combined with PD-L1 inhibitor (durvalumab) to restore T cell function and enhance antitumor immunity
Validated.Unnamed: 72: Anti-CTLA-4 monoclonal antibody that blocks the inhibitory signal resulting from CTLA-4 binding to CD80/86, leading to prolongation and enhancement of T-cell activation and expansion. This results in increased activation and expansion of tumor antigen-specific cytotoxic T cells (CTLs) and may reduce the suppressive activity of regulatory T cells (Tregs) in the tumor microenvironment.
Validated.Unnamed: 73: EGFR-TKI inhibits EGFR signaling pathways by competitively binding to the intracellular tyrosine kinase domain, blocking ATP binding and subsequent autophosphorylation, which leads to inhibition of downstream signaling cascades (such as AKT, MAPK, and JNK) and ultimately blocks tumor cell proliferation and survival. Platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy induces DNA damage and cell death in rapidly dividing cancer cells. The combination enhances anti-tumor efficacy through synergistic effects, potentially overcoming or delaying resistance.
Validated.Unnamed: 74: Crizotinib is a tyrosine kinase inhibitor that binds to the ATP-binding pocket of the abnormal ALK protein, blocking its kinase activity and thereby inhibiting downstream signaling pathways involved in cell proliferation and survival. It also inhibits c-Met (HGFR) and ROS1 kinases, leading to reduced tumor growth and survival.
Validated.Unnamed: 75: Therapeutic cancer vaccine targeting α-lactalbumin, a lactation-associated protein typically expressed only in breast tissue during lactation but re-expressed in many forms of breast cancer including TNBC. The vaccine activates the immune system to direct cytotoxic T cells against tumor cells expressing α-lactalbumin, providing preemptive immune protection against emerging breast tumors. Induces α-lactalbumin-specific T cell responses with interferon gamma and interleukin-17 production.
Validated.Unnamed: 76: N/A - comparing management strategies (active monitoring vs. surgery), not drug mechanisms

Validated.Unnamed: 41: Cabozantinib (CABOMETYX) is approved for advanced neuroendocrine tumors. Other approved tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKIs) for neuroendocrine tumors include sunitinib (Sutent) and everolimus (Afinitor). Notable investigational agents in development include surufatinib (a multi-targeted TKI), lenvatinib (Lenvima), and various peptide receptor radionuclide therapies (PRRT) such as lutetium Lu 177 dotatate (Lutathera). Additionally, belzutifan has recently been approved for pheochromocytoma and paraganglioma, which are rare neuroendocrine tumors.

Validated.Unnamed: 46: Bevacizumab, cetuximab, panitumumab, ziv-aflibercept, ramucirumab, and investigational agents such as tucatinib + trastuzumab (MOUNTAINEER-03 trial), FOLFOXIRI-bevacizumab, and other EGFR and VEGF inhibitors in development. Additionally, encorafenib + cetuximab is now FDA-approved for BRAF V600E-mutated mCRC, and other BRAF/EGFR/MEK inhibitor combinations are in development.

Validated.Unnamed: 52: Pembrolizumab, nivolumab, relatlimab (Opdualag), ipilimumab, cemiplimab, and other PD-1/LAG-3 or PD-1/CTLA-4 checkpoint inhibitor combinations are approved or in development for advanced melanoma. Notable KRAS G12D inhibitors in development include VS-7375, TSN1611, and ARV-806.

Validated.Unnamed: 65: Pembrolizumab (approved PD-1 inhibitor for dMMR/MSI-H cancers), nivolumab (approved PD-1 inhibitor), ipilimumab (approved CTLA-4 inhibitor, used in combination with nivolumab for dMMR/MSI-H colorectal cancer), and other investigational PD-1 inhibitors in development for dMMR/MSI-H cancers
Validated.Unnamed: 66: Tarlatamab (approved DLL3 bispecific for ES-SCLC), lurbinectedin (approved for relapsed SCLC), topotecan (approved for relapsed SCLC), nivolumab (approved for SCLC maintenance), pembrolizumab (approved in combination for ES-SCLC), AMG 757 (DLL3 bispecific in development), datopotamab deruxtecan (B7-H3 ADC in development), and other B7-H3 and DLL3-targeted therapies in development
Validated.Unnamed: 67: Lurbinectedin (approved for 2nd-line ES-SCLC), tarlatamab (DLL3 bispecific, approved 2024), topotecan (traditional 2nd-line option). Other TROP2-targeting ADCs in development include datopotamab deruxtecan and OBI-902. DLL3-targeting bispecifics in development include ifinatamab deruxtecan, HPN328, and BI 764532.
Validated.Unnamed: 68: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (Enhertu, approved HER2-targeting ADC for HER2-mutant NSCLC), poziotinib (HER2 TKI in development), and other investigational HER2-targeting agents in clinical trials
Validated.Unnamed: 69: Osimertinib (approved 1st-line EGFR TKI), amivantamab (EGFR x MET bispecific antibody), patritumab deruxtecan (HER3 ADC in development), other EGFR x HER3 bispecific ADCs in development (AVZO-1418/DB-1418), sunvozertinib (approved for EGFR exon 20 insertion NSCLC), sacituzumab tirumotecan (TROP2 ADC in development for EGFR TKI-resistant NSCLC), and other EGFR TKI + chemotherapy combinations (e.g., FLAURA2 regimen)
Validated.Unnamed: 70: Trastuzumab, pertuzumab, trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1), trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd), trastuzumab rezetecan, zongertinib, tucatinib, neratinib, lapatinib, margetuximab, zanidatamab, and other HER2-targeted therapies (including monoclonal antibodies, antibody-drug conjugates, and kinase inhibitors) are approved or in development for HER2-positive breast cancer.
Validated.Unnamed: 71: Docetaxel (approved 2nd-line NSCLC), other checkpoint inhibitors (pembrolizumab, nivolumab, atezolizumab), other ATR inhibitors in development (berzosertib, elimusertib)
Validated.Unnamed: 72:
Approved: Ipilimumab (Yervoy, approved CTLA-4 inhibitor), docetaxel, pemetrexed, ramucirumab + docetaxel
In-trial: Other anti-CTLA-4 antibodies in development, novel immunotherapy combinations (including combinations with PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors, LAG-3 inhibitors, and other immune modulators)
Validated.Unnamed: 73: Gefitinib, erlotinib, afatinib, dacomitinib (approved EGFR-TKIs); osimertinib (approved third-generation EGFR-TKI); lazertinib, amivantamab (approved combinations); sunvozertinib (approved for EGFR exon 20 insertion); sacituzumab tirumotecan (investigational TROP2 ADC in combination with EGFR-TKIs); other EGFR-TKI combinations and fourth-generation EGFR-TKIs (e.g., JIN-A02) in development
Validated.Unnamed: 74: Alectinib (approved ALK inhibitor, adjuvant setting), brigatinib (approved ALK inhibitor), lorlatinib (approved ALK inhibitor), ceritinib (approved ALK inhibitor), ensartinib (investigational ALK inhibitor), and next-generation agents in development (e.g., agents designed to reduce CNS toxicity while preserving efficacy).
Validated.Unnamed: 75: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) and nivolumab (Opdivo) - approved PD-1 inhibitors for TNBC in combination with chemotherapy; atezolizumab (Tecentriq) - approved PD-L1 inhibitor for PD-L1-positive metastatic TNBC; other investigational cancer vaccines and immunotherapies in development for breast cancer prevention and treatment.
Validated.Unnamed: 76: Tamoxifen, anastrozole, letrozole, exemestane (approved hormone therapies for hormone receptor-positive DCIS); ongoing trials (LORD, LORIS, LORETTA, RECAST, TBCC-ARO DCIS, SentiNot 2.0) evaluating active surveillance, low-dose tamoxifen, or delayed sentinel lymph node dissection with or without endocrine therapy

Validated.Unnamed: 2: Single-agent chemotherapy (eribulin, vinorelbine, capecitabine, or gemcitabine) for HR+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer patients who have progressed on endocrine therapy
Validated.Unnamed: 3: CDK4/6 inhibitor (palbociclib, ribociclib, or abemaciclib) plus aromatase inhibitor for first-line HR+/HER2- advanced breast cancer
Validated.Unnamed: 4: CDK4/6 inhibitor combined with aromatase inhibitor (AI) for postmenopausal women, or AI plus ovarian suppression for premenopausal women
Validated.Unnamed: 5: Pembrolizumab + chemotherapy for PD-L1-positive (CPS ≥10) mTNBC; chemotherapy alone for PD-L1-negative or ICI-ineligible patients
Validated.Unnamed: 6: Pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy for PD-L1+ (CPS ≥10) locally recurrent unresectable or metastatic triple-negative breast cancer
Validated.Unnamed: 7: 2nd-line: Liposomal irinotecan/5-FU/leucovorin, FOLFOX, or modified FOLFIRINOX
Validated.Unnamed: 9: Pembrolizumab plus platinum-based chemotherapy for first-line metastatic NSCLC, regardless of PD-L1 status
Validated.Unnamed: 10: Atezolizumab plus bevacizumab is the standard of care for first-line treatment of unresectable or metastatic HCC
Validated.Unnamed: 11: Adjuvant capecitabine for 6 months (based on BILCAP trial)
Validated.Unnamed: 13: 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine + daunorubicin or idarubicin) followed by consolidation with high-dose cytarabine
Validated.Unnamed: 14: Pembrolizumab + platinum-based chemotherapy (KEYNOTE-189 regimen) for first-line advanced non-squamous NSCLC. For HER2-mutant NSCLC specifically, zongertinib and T-DXd are approved for second-line and later therapy
Validated.Unnamed: 15: For advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) refractory to anti-PD-(L)1 and platinum-based chemotherapy, the current standard of care is docetaxel chemotherapy, often used as a single agent in the second-line or later setting
Validated.Unnamed: 16: Docetaxel chemotherapy remains a standard second-line or later treatment for advanced NSCLC after progression on immune checkpoint inhibitors, though immunotherapy combinations (e.g., nivolumab plus ipilimumab, durvalumab plus chemotherapy) are increasingly used in earlier lines.
Validated.Unnamed: 17: For advanced NSCLC previously treated with ICIs and chemotherapy, the standard of care is varied and includes chemotherapy rechallenge, immunotherapy rechallenge, or targeted therapy based on molecular profile. Pragmatic trial designs allow flexibility, reflecting real-world practice where treatment choice depends on patient characteristics and prior therapies.
Validated.Unnamed: 18: Physician's choice chemotherapy (capecitabine, paclitaxel, or nab-paclitaxel)
Validated.Unnamed: 19: T-DXd (Enhertu) is the current standard of care in 2nd-line HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer after trastuzumab/taxane failure, having shown superiority over T-DM1 in DESTINY-Breast003; first-line standard is taxane plus trastuzumab and pertuzumab (THP)
Validated.Unnamed: 20: Fulvestrant + palbociclib doublet
Validated.Unnamed: 21: Lapatinib + capecitabine (standard second-line regimen after trastuzumab progression); T-DXd increasingly preferred based on recent Phase 3 data
Validated.Unnamed: 22: mFOLFOX6, FOLFOXIRI, or CAPOX ± bevacizumab
Validated.Unnamed: 23: mFOLFOX6 chemotherapy alone for adjuvant treatment of stage III dMMR/MSI-high colorectal cancer
Validated.Unnamed: 24: Surgery followed by adjuvant chemotherapy (FOLFOX or CAPOX) for stage II-III colorectal cancer
Validated.Unnamed: 26: For advanced melanoma, the current standard of care is pembrolizumab or nivolumab monotherapy as first-line treatment. For patients with BRAF wild-type tumors, combination therapy with nivolumab plus ipilimumab is also an option, particularly for those who can tolerate higher toxicity for potentially greater benefit.
Validated.Unnamed: 27: Pembrolizumab monotherapy or nivolumab monotherapy, with nivolumab plus ipilimumab as an alternative for patients who may benefit from combination immunotherapy
Validated.Unnamed: 28: Abiraterone acetate + prednisone is the standard of care for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) and is also used in metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (mCSPC). For mCSPC with HRR mutations, the combination of niraparib + abiraterone acetate + prednisone is now approved and considered a standard option.
Validated.Unnamed: 29: External beam radiation therapy (EBRT) combined with androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) for high-risk localized prostate cancer, with ADT duration typically ranging from 18 to 36 months based on risk stratification and radiotherapy approach.
Validated.Unnamed: 30: ADT alone for biochemical recurrence; observation or salvage radiotherapy may also be considered depending on risk factors
Validated.Unnamed: 31: For extensive-stage SCLC, the standard of care is induction with platinum-based chemotherapy (carboplatin or cisplatin) plus etoposide, combined with an immune checkpoint inhibitor (atezolizumab or durvalumab), followed by maintenance with the same immune checkpoint inhibitor if there is no progression
Validated.Unnamed: 33: For HER2-low metastatic breast cancer after prior therapy, standard of care is physician's choice chemotherapy (capecitabine, eribulin, gemcitabine, or taxanes). For HER2-positive disease, first-line standard is taxane plus trastuzumab and pertuzumab.
Validated.Unnamed: 34: CDK4/6 inhibitor combined with endocrine therapy (aromatase inhibitor or fulvestrant) for first-line treatment; alpelisib plus fulvestrant for PIK3CA-mutated patients after progression on endocrine therapy
Validated.Unnamed: 35: For relapsed/refractory follicular lymphoma, standard of care includes rituximab plus lenalidomide (R2), bendamustine plus rituximab, CHOP or CVP-based chemoimmunotherapy, or other anti-CD20 antibody-based regimens. Treatment selection depends on prior therapies, response duration, and patient factors.
Validated.Unnamed: 36: Somatostatin analogs (octreotide, lanreotide) for symptom control and disease stabilization; 177Lu-DOTATATE (Lutathera) for eligible patients with progressive disease; everolimus or sunitinib for progressive disease.
Validated.Unnamed: 37: Neoadjuvant cisplatin-based chemotherapy + radical cystectomy (for cisplatin-eligible); immediate radical cystectomy (for cisplatin-ineligible)
Validated.Unnamed: 38: Second-line chemotherapy options: topotecan, lurbinectedin, or amrubicin (investigator's choice); for relapsed SCLC with a chemotherapy-free interval <90 days, single-agent systemic therapy (topotecan, lurbinectedin, or tarlatamab) is preferred; for relapsed SCLC with a chemotherapy-free interval ≥90 days, rechallenge with platinum-based regimen or single-agent systemic therapy (topotecan, lurbinectedin, or tarlatamab) may be offered
Validated.Unnamed: 39: For metastatic HR+ breast cancer, standard of care is endocrine therapy, typically aromatase inhibitors (AIs) or fulvestrant, often combined with CDK4/6 inhibitors in the first-line setting. For early-stage HR+ breast cancer, standard of care is 5-10 years of endocrine therapy (tamoxifen, AIs, or sequential therapy) after surgery.
Validated.Unnamed: 40: Adjuvant endocrine therapy with aromatase inhibitors (anastrozole, letrozole, exemestane) for postmenopausal women or tamoxifen for premenopausal women, typically for 5-10 years
Validated.Unnamed: 41: For previously treated, progressive advanced neuroendocrine tumors (pancreatic and extra-pancreatic), the standard of care is typically a tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI) such as sunitinib or everolimus for pancreatic NETs, or everolimus for extra-pancreatic NETs. For patients who have progressed on prior TKIs, options may include peptide receptor radionuclide therapy (PRRT) with lutetium Lu 177 dotatate, or chemotherapy regimens such as capecitabine and temozolomide. Cabozantinib is now an approved option for second-line or later therapy, and belzutifan is approved for pheochromocytoma and paraganglioma.
Validated.Unnamed: 42: CDK4/6 inhibitors combined with endocrine therapy (such as aromatase inhibitors or fulvestrant) is the standard first-line treatment for hormone receptor-positive (HR+), HER2-negative (HER2-) advanced or metastatic breast cancer. For early breast cancer, CDK4/6 inhibitors (abemaciclib, ribociclib) are approved in specific high-risk populations in combination with endocrine therapy.
Validated.Unnamed: 43: For relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma after 1-4 prior lines, standard regimens include combinations of immunomodulatory drugs (lenalidomide, pomalidomide), proteasome inhibitors (bortezomib, carfilzomib), anti-CD38 antibodies (daratumumab, isatuximab), and corticosteroids. Increasingly, bispecific antibodies (teclistamab, elranatamab, linvoseltamab) and CAR-T therapies (cilta-cel, ide-cel) are used in later lines, especially after multiple prior therapies.
Validated.Unnamed: 44: BTK inhibitors (ibrutinib, acalabrutinib, zanubrutinib) as monotherapy or venetoclax-based combinations for most patients; chemoimmunotherapy (bendamustine-rituximab, FCR) for select fit patients without high-risk features
Validated.Unnamed: 45: CDK4/6 inhibitor plus endocrine therapy (aromatase inhibitor or fulvestrant) in 1st-line; endocrine therapy (fulvestrant, aromatase inhibitor, or exemestane) plus everolimus in 2nd-line post-CDK4/6 inhibitor progression
Validated.Unnamed: 46: For patients with metastatic colorectal cancer, the current standard of care is mFOLFOX6 or FOLFIRI chemotherapy combined with either bevacizumab (anti-VEGF) or cetuximab/panitumumab (anti-EGFR) for those with RAS wild-type tumors. For BRAF V600E-mutated tumors, encorafenib + cetuximab is now FDA-approved in the first-line setting.
Validated.Unnamed: 47: For newly diagnosed H3K27M-mutant diffuse glioma, the standard of care is radiation therapy (typically 54 Gy focal radiotherapy). Until recently, there were no effective systemic therapies beyond radiation. With FDA accelerated approval of dordaviprone in August 2025, it is now available as the first systemic therapy option for recurrent disease. Median overall survival historically is approximately 1 year from diagnosis.
Validated.Unnamed: 48: R-CHOP (rituximab plus cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine, and prednisone) for first-line treatment of DLBCL
Validated.Unnamed: 49: Chemotherapy, surgery, and radiation therapy as indicated; olaparib is now approved as adjuvant therapy for this population based on OlympiA trial results
Validated.Unnamed: 50: mFOLFOX6 alone (historical standard); atezolizumab + mFOLFOX6 (new standard of care based on ATOMIC trial results, ASCO 2025)
Validated.Unnamed: 51: First-line: Pembrolizumab monotherapy or nivolumab + ipilimumab (both FDA-approved 2025); chemotherapy (FOLFOX/FOLFIRI ± bevacizumab/cetuximab) is alternative option
Validated.Unnamed: 52: For previously untreated patients with advanced or metastatic melanoma, the current standard of care is combination immunotherapy with a PD-1 inhibitor (e.g., pembrolizumab or nivolumab) plus a CTLA-4 inhibitor (ipilimumab) or a PD-1/LAG-3 inhibitor combination (e.g., relatlimab + nivolumab, Opdualag). For BRAF V600 mutation-positive patients, targeted therapy with BRAF/MEK inhibitors is also an option.
Validated.Unnamed: 53: Anthracycline and taxane-based neoadjuvant chemotherapy, often with platinum agents (carboplatin), for high-risk early-stage TNBC
Validated.Unnamed: 54: For MSI-H/dMMR metastatic colorectal cancer, pembrolizumab monotherapy is now the standard of care for first-line treatment. For patients not eligible for immunotherapy, FOLFOX or FOLFIRI chemotherapy ± bevacizumab or cetuximab remains an option. For potentially resectable MSI-H/dMMR CRC, neoadjuvant immunotherapy with PD-1 inhibitors is increasingly recommended.
Validated.Unnamed: 55: R-CHOP (rituximab plus cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine, and prednisone) for most patients with previously untreated DLBCL
Validated.Unnamed: 56: Osimertinib plus platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy as first-line treatment for EGFR-mutated (exon 19del or L858R) advanced NSCLC, based on FLAURA2 trial results and FDA approval
Validated.Unnamed: 57: Cisplatin-based neoadjuvant chemotherapy (typically gemcitabine + cisplatin) followed by radical cystectomy
Validated.Unnamed: 58: Adjuvant chemotherapy (anthracycline and taxane-based regimens) for high-risk TNBC; pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy in neoadjuvant setting for PD-L1 positive tumors; platinum-based chemotherapy for BRCA-mutated TNBC; sacituzumab govitecan for advanced TNBC not eligible for immune checkpoint inhibitors
Validated.Unnamed: 59: For relapsed/refractory mantle cell lymphoma after BTK inhibitor and anti-CD20 therapy, standard options include alternative BTK inhibitors (if not previously used), chemotherapy regimens (bendamustine, cytarabine-based), venetoclax-based combinations, and CAR-T cell therapy (brexucabtagene autoleucel) in eligible patients
Validated.Unnamed: 60: Maximum safe surgical resection followed by concurrent radiotherapy (60 Gy in 30 fractions) and temozolomide (75 mg/m²/day for 6 weeks), then adjuvant temozolomide (150-200 mg/m²/day for 5 days every 28 days for up to 6 cycles).
Validated.Unnamed: 61: CDK4/6 inhibitors combined with endocrine therapy (aromatase inhibitors or fulvestrant) are the standard first-line treatment for hormone receptor-positive, HER2-negative advanced or metastatic breast cancer, and are increasingly used in the adjuvant setting for high-risk early breast cancer.
Validated.Unnamed: 62: Surgery and chemotherapy (neoadjuvant or adjuvant), with endocrine therapy for hormone receptor-positive disease, and PARP inhibitors (olaparib or talazoparib) for patients with germline BRCA1/2 mutations in the adjuvant or metastatic setting.
Validated.Unnamed: 63: For patients with KRAS G12C-mutated locally advanced or metastatic NSCLC who have received prior chemotherapy and immunotherapy, KRAS G12C inhibitors such as sotorasib or adagrasib are approved treatment options. Other options may include docetaxel or other chemotherapy regimens.
Validated.Unnamed: 64: For patients with advanced or metastatic nonsquamous NSCLC harboring EGFR mutations who have progressed following a tyrosine kinase inhibitor and platinum-based chemotherapy, the current standard of care includes chemotherapy (docetaxel or pemetrexed) or targeted therapy with amivantamab (for EGFR exon20 insertion mutations) or mobocertinib (for EGFR exon20 insertion mutations)
Validated.Unnamed: 65: Traditional standard: neoadjuvant chemoradiotherapy followed by surgery. Emerging practice: neoadjuvant PD-1 inhibitor monotherapy (based on recent breakthrough data showing high rates of clinical complete response in dMMR/MSI-H rectal cancer, with some studies reporting 100% clinical complete response rates)
Validated.Unnamed: 66: For patients with extensive-stage small cell lung cancer who have progressed after first-line platinum-based chemotherapy, standard options include topotecan, lurbinectedin, or tarlatamab (approved May 2024). Immunotherapy rechallenge may be considered if not used in first-line. Clinical trial participation is strongly encouraged given limited efficacy of available options.
Validated.Unnamed: 67: For 2nd-line ES-SCLC, current standard of care options include topotecan, lurbinectedin (approved 2020), or rechallenge with platinum-based chemotherapy. Tarlatamab (DLL3 bispecific) was recently approved in 2024 for relapsed ES-SCLC.
Validated.Unnamed: 68: Platinum-based chemotherapy; trastuzumab deruxtecan (Enhertu) is approved for HER2-mutant NSCLC
Validated.Unnamed: 69: For first-line EGFR-mutated NSCLC, the current standard of care is third-generation EGFR TKI monotherapy (osimertinib) or first/second-generation EGFR TKIs (erlotinib, gefitinib, afatinib). Combination therapy with osimertinib plus chemotherapy is also emerging as a standard of care based on recent trial data.
Validated.Unnamed: 70: For first-line treatment of HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer, the current standard of care remains the combination of taxane chemotherapy (e.g., paclitaxel or docetaxel), trastuzumab, and pertuzumab (THP regimen). However, recent data from the DESTINY-Breast09 trial demonstrate that trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) plus pertuzumab is superior to THP in terms of progression-free survival and response rates, and is poised to become the new standard of care pending regulatory approval and guideline updates.
Validated.Unnamed: 71: Docetaxel chemotherapy for patients with advanced NSCLC who have progressed on anti-PD-(L)1 therapy and platinum-based chemotherapy
Validated.Unnamed: 72: For metastatic NSCLC that has progressed on PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor therapy, the current standard of care is typically docetaxel-based chemotherapy (with or without ramucirumab), pemetrexed (for non-squamous), or other platinum-based chemotherapy regimens. Clinical trial enrollment is also commonly considered at this stage.
Validated.Unnamed: 73: For EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC, the current standard of care is first-line EGFR-TKI monotherapy, with osimertinib being the preferred third-generation TKI. The FLAURA2 trial results have established osimertinib plus platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy as a new standard of care for first-line treatment, offering a significant improvement in overall survival compared to osimertinib monotherapy.
Validated.Unnamed: 74: For adjuvant treatment of ALK-positive NSCLC post-surgery, the current standard of care is adjuvant ALK inhibitor therapy (alectinib preferred based on ALINA trial results) following complete surgical resection. Adjuvant chemotherapy may also be considered. Observation was previously standard but is being replaced by targeted therapy.
Validated.Unnamed: 75: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy is the standard of care for most patients with clinically node-positive disease and for a large proportion of triple-negative and HER2-positive breast cancers. For high-risk, early-stage ER+/HER2- breast cancer, neoadjuvant chemotherapy is also standard, with pembrolizumab increasingly added for improved outcomes. For high-risk, early-stage TNBC, neoadjuvant chemotherapy plus pembrolizumab followed by adjuvant pembrolizumab is now standard.
Validated.Unnamed: 76: Surgery (breast-conserving surgery or mastectomy) is the standard initial treatment for DCIS. For most patients, this is followed by radiation therapy and/or hormone therapy (tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitors for hormone receptor-positive cases). For low-risk DCIS, active surveillance is emerging as an alternative, but surgery remains the standard.

Validated.Unnamed: 2: Trodelvy/sacituzumab govitecan (ASCENT trials in HR+/HER2- breast cancer), T-DXd/trastuzumab deruxtecan (DESTINY-Breast04, NCT03734029 in HER2-low breast cancer), other TROP2 ADCs in development
Validated.Unnamed: 3: EMERALD (elacestrant), EMBER-3 (imlunestrant), and other oral SERD trials (giredestrant, amcenestrant) in HR+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer with ESR1 mutations
Validated.Unnamed: 4: EMBER-3 (imlunestrant + CDK4/6), other oral SERD trials (elacestrant, giredestrant)
Validated.Unnamed: 5: ASCENT-04/KEYNOTE-D19 (sacituzumab govitecan + pembrolizumab, NCT05382286), TROPION-Breast01 (datopotamab deruxtecan, NCT05374512), KEYNOTE-355 (pembrolizumab + chemotherapy, established standard)
Validated.Unnamed: 6: Datopotamab deruxtecan (Dato-DXd) combinations (e.g., TROPION-Breast01, NCT05153330), trastuzumab deruxtecan (DESTINY-Breast06, NCT04468061), other ADC + immunotherapy combinations in TNBC
Validated.Unnamed: 7: KRAS G12C inhibitors (adagrasib NCT05634525), KRAS G12D inhibitors (MRTX1133, zoldonrasib), other pan-RAS inhibitors in Phase 1/2
Validated.Unnamed: 8: JDQ443; other RAS drugs; Phase Ib trial of adagrasib + olaparib (NCT06130254); Phase 1/2 trial of avutometinib + adagrasib; trials of FMC-376, BBO-11818, elironrasib, garsorasib
Validated.Unnamed: 9: Pembrolizumab + chemo (KEYNOTE-189, KEYNOTE-407); sugemalimab + chemo (GEMSTONE-302); domvanalimab + zimberelimab + chemo (STAR-121, NCT05502237); tiragolumab + atezolizumab + chemo (SKYSCRAPER-01, terminated); vibostolimab + pembrolizumab (KeyVibe-010, terminated); ociperlimab + tislelizumab (AdvanTIG-302, terminated)
Validated.Unnamed: 11: Other adjuvant immunotherapy trials in biliary tract cancer (e.g., durvalumab-based combinations); BILCAP established current capecitabine standard
Validated.Unnamed: 13: Standard chemotherapy protocols (7+3), menin inhibitor trials (Revumenib NCT07211958, NCT06652438), other targeted therapies in development
Validated.Unnamed: 14: Zongertinib (Beamion LUNG-1 trial), DESTINY-Lung04 trial (T-DXd), and other investigational HER2-targeted TKIs and ADCs in development
Validated.Unnamed: 15: Other Phase 3 trials in 2nd-line+ NSCLC post-immunotherapy include various targeted therapies and novel combinations. Specific competing trials in this exact population (post-anti-PD-L1 and platinum) are limited, with docetaxel remaining the primary comparator. Other ATR inhibitor combinations (berzosertib, camonsertib) are in earlier-phase development.
Validated.Unnamed: 16: Major competing therapies and trials include nivolumab plus ipilimumab (CheckMate 227, CheckMate 9LA), durvalumab plus tremelimumab (POSEIDON), and other post-ICI NSCLC trials evaluating novel CTLA-4 agents and PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors in combination with chemotherapy or other agents.
Validated.Unnamed: 17: Other ICI + anti-angiogenic combinations (bevacizumab + atezolizumab, axitinib + pembrolizumab), chemotherapy rechallenge regimens, and other 2nd-line NSCLC trials evaluating novel combinations
Validated.Unnamed: 18: Other HER2-targeted ADCs in development for HER2-low disease; endocrine-based therapies with novel agents
Validated.Unnamed: 19: DESTINY-Breast03 (T-DXd vs T-DM1, showed T-DXd superiority), DESTINY-Breast05 (T-DXd in early breast cancer), DESTINY-Breast09 (T-DXd + pertuzumab in 1st-line), other HER2 ADCs in development
Validated.Unnamed: 20: VIKTORIA-1 (gedatolisib + fulvestrant ± palbociclib), CAPItello-291 (capivasertib + fulvestrant), other PI3K/AKT pathway inhibitor trials
Validated.Unnamed: 21: DESTINY-Breast02 (T-DXd vs chemotherapy), DESTINY-Breast03 (T-DXd vs T-DM1), other HER2-targeted ADCs and TKIs in development
Validated.Unnamed: 22: Other BRAF V600E-targeted regimens (e.g., encorafenib + cetuximab without chemotherapy, other BRAF/EGFR inhibitor combinations)
Validated.Unnamed: 23: AZUR-2 trial (dostarlimab neoadjuvant/adjuvant), AZUR-4 trial (dostarlimab + CAPEOX vs CAPEOX alone), Superhero trial (dostarlimab before surgery for colon cancer)
Validated.Unnamed: 24: No direct competing trials for low-dose aspirin in adjuvant PI3K-altered colorectal cancer; CALGB/SWOG 80702 evaluated celecoxib in similar population
Validated.Unnamed: 26: Key competing therapies include nivolumab plus ipilimumab, pembrolizumab, nivolumab, cemiplimab, tebentafusp, and investigational agents such as mRNA-4359 + pembrolizumab and EVX-01 + pembrolizumab. Notable trials include CheckMate 76K (nivolumab), KEYNOTE-006 (pembrolizumab), and ongoing studies evaluating novel combinations and agents.
Validated.Unnamed: 28: Competing approaches: Other PARP inhibitor combinations (olaparib, rucaparib, talazoparib) with AR inhibitors; AR inhibitor intensification (enzalutamide, apalutamide, darolutamide) + ADT; docetaxel + ADT. Key trial: MAGNITUDE (NCT03748641) for mCRPC with similar combination.
Validated.Unnamed: 29: Standard radiation approaches (EBRT with or without brachytherapy boost), radical prostatectomy, various radiation fractionation schedules (conventional vs. hypofractionated), and combination approaches with ADT of varying durations. Other investigational approaches include alternative oncolytic virus therapies and immune checkpoint inhibitors in development for localized prostate cancer.
Validated.Unnamed: 30: Other AR inhibitor trials in biochemical recurrence setting; EMBARK trial (enzalutamide in high-risk nmHSPC)
Validated.Unnamed: 31: Major competing therapies and trials include durvalumab (Imfinzi) as maintenance after chemoradiotherapy for limited-stage SCLC, tarlatamab (Imdelltra) for relapsed SCLC, and ongoing trials such as DeLLphi-304 (tarlatamab vs chemotherapy), DeLLphi-305 (tarlatamab + durvalumab vs durvalumab alone), and DeLLphi-312 (tarlatamab as induction and maintenance in first-line ES-SCLC)
Validated.Unnamed: 33: DESTINY-Breast06 (trastuzumab deruxtecan in HER2-low first-line), KATHERINE (trastuzumab emtansine adjuvant), other HER2-targeted ADCs in development for HER2-low disease
Validated.Unnamed: 34: Alpelisib (SOLAR-1, NCT02437318), Capivasertib (CAPItello-291, NCT04862663), Elacestrant (EMERALD, NCT03778931), Camizestrant (SERENA-6, NCT04214518), Other PI3K inhibitor trials in PIK3CA-mutated HR+/HER2- breast cancer
Validated.Unnamed: 35: Other CD20×CD3 bispecifics (glofitamab, mosunetuzumab, odronextamab) in various FL trials, CAR-T therapies being evaluated in FL (axicabtagene ciloleucel, tisagenlecleucel), tazemetostat for EZH2-mutant FL, PI3K inhibitors (idelalisib, copanlisib, parsaclisib, zandelisib, TQ-B3525), and various combination immunotherapy trials in R/R FL
Validated.Unnamed: 36: 177Lu-DOTATATE (Lutathera - approved), everolimus, sunitinib. Competing trials: NETTER-2 (177Lu-DOTATATE in advanced GEP-NETs), COMPOSE trial (177Lu-edotreotide in aggressive Grade 2/3 GEP-NETs), and several other ongoing trials evaluating 177Lu-DOTATATE in various solid tumors.
Validated.Unnamed: 37: Durvalumab (POTOMAC trial NCT03732677 in MIBC), pembrolizumab (KEYNOTE-866 NCT03924895 arm A), atezolizumab (IMvigor010 NCT02450331 adjuvant), nivolumab (CheckMate 274 NCT02632409 adjuvant), enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab (EV-304/KEYNOTE-B15 NCT03924895), disitamab vedotin + pembrolizumab (NCT05338777), BT8009 + pembrolizumab (NCT04577238)
Validated.Unnamed: 38: DeLLphi-301 (tarlatamab Phase 2, NCT05060016), IDeate-Lung01 (ifinatamab deruxtecan DLL3-targeting ADC, NCT05280470), other DLL3-targeting bispecific and ADC programs in development for SCLC, ADRIATIC (durvalumab consolidation in limited-stage SCLC), NRG-LU005, KEYLYNK-013, ACHILES (immunotherapy trials in SCLC)
Validated.Unnamed: 39: Elacestrant (EMERALD trial, NCT03778931), Camizestrant (SERENA-4 trial, NCT04711252), Giredestrant (evERA Breast Cancer, NCT04546009), Amcenestrant (AMEERA trials)
Validated.Unnamed: 40: Elacestrant (EMERALD trial, NCT03778931), Camizestrant (SERENA-4 trial), Imlunestrant (EMBER-3 trial), Lasofoxifene (ELAINE trials)
Validated.Unnamed: 41: Key competing drugs include sunitinib (Sutent), everolimus (Afinitor), and lutetium Lu 177 dotatate (Lutathera). Notable ongoing trials include the Phase 3 SURUFATINIB trial (NCT02549937) evaluating surufatinib for advanced neuroendocrine tumors, the Phase 3 NETTER-2 trial (NCT03972488) evaluating lutetium Lu 177 dotatate in earlier lines of therapy, and the Phase 2 study of lenvatinib with everolimus. Other trials include those evaluating belzutifan for pheochromocytoma and paraganglioma, and various chemotherapy combinations.
Validated.Unnamed: 42: INAVO120, INAVO121, INAVO122 trials (inavolisib), EMBRACE (elacestrant), EMBER-3 (imlunestrant), PADA-1 (palbociclib + fulvestrant), PACE (palbociclib + fulvestrant), PENELOPE-B (palbociclib), NATALEE (ribociclib), monarchE (abemaciclib), CAPItello-291 (capivasertib), BYLieve (alpelisib), NCT04216472 (giredestrant), NCT04216472 (camizestrant), NCT04216472 (amcenestrant), NCT04216472 (AZD9833), NCT04216472 (AZD9496), NCT04216472 (onapristone), NCT04216472 (ZN-c3)
Validated.Unnamed: 43: Key competing drugs: Teclistamab (Tecvayli), elranatamab (Elrexfio), linvoseltamab (Lynozyfic), talquetamab, cilta-cel (Carvykti), ide-cel (Abecma). Key competing trials: MajesTEC-1 (teclistamab monotherapy in RRMM), MagnetisMM-3 (elranatamab vs standard regimens in RRMM), MonumenTAL-1 (talquetamab in RRMM), CARTITUDE-4 (cilta-cel in earlier RRMM), KarMMa-3 (ide-cel in RRMM), and ongoing trials for P-BCMA-ALLO1 and other bispecifics.
Validated.Unnamed: 44: BRUIN CLL-313 (NCT05023980, pirtobrutinib vs bendamustine-rituximab in treatment-naïve CLL/SLL), BRUIN CLL-321 (NCT04666038, pirtobrutinib vs idelalisib-rituximab or bendamustine-rituximab in BTK inhibitor-pretreated), ELEVATE-TN (acalabrutinib trials), SEQUOIA (zanubrutinib trials)
Validated.Unnamed: 45: Elacestrant (EMERALD trial - approved), Camizestrant (SERENA-4 trial), Imlunestrant (EMBER-3 trial)
Validated.Unnamed: 46: BEACON CRC (encorafenib + cetuximab in later-line BRAF V600E mCRC), FOLFOXIRI-bevacizumab trials in first-line mCRC, and other trials evaluating BRAF/EGFR/MEK inhibitor combinations. Additionally, CheckMate 8HW (ipilimumab + nivolumab) is a new standard of care for MSI-H/dMMR mCRC.
Validated.Unnamed: 47: Limited competing Phase 3 trials exist for H3K27M-mutant diffuse glioma due to the rarity of the disease. Other investigational approaches include: ONC206 (next-generation imipridone in earlier phase studies), various immunotherapy approaches (peptide vaccines, CAR-T therapies in early phase), and targeted therapies. The ACTION trial (NCT05580562) is the primary Phase 3 study in newly diagnosed patients.
Validated.Unnamed: 48: Other CD20xCD3 bispecifics in development for first-line DLBCL (glofitamab, epcoritamab, mosunetuzumab in various Phase 3 trials); CAR-T therapies being evaluated in earlier lines; polatuzumab vedotin combinations
Validated.Unnamed: 49: Talazoparib (PARP inhibitor approved for metastatic setting); other adjuvant trials include CDK4/6 inhibitors (abemaciclib, ribociclib) in HR+ disease
Validated.Unnamed: 50: Neoadjuvant immunotherapy approaches (NICHE trial); pembrolizumab in metastatic dMMR setting (KEYNOTE-177); other PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors in development for adjuvant dMMR colon cancer
Validated.Unnamed: 51: Pembrolizumab (KEYNOTE-177, approved first-line); dostarlimab trials in MSI-H/dMMR CRC (e.g., NCT04581824); other PD-1/PD-L1 combination trials in development
Validated.Unnamed: 52: Key competing therapies include pembrolizumab + ipilimumab, nivolumab + ipilimumab, and relatlimab + nivolumab (Opdualag). Notable ongoing Phase 3 trials include NCT05539622 (tebentafusp), NCT04669051 (lifileucel), and NCT05217446 (bispecific antibodies targeting LAG-3/PD-1).
Validated.Unnamed: 53: IMpassion031 (atezolizumab + chemotherapy in early-stage TNBC), ASCENT (sacituzumab govitecan in metastatic TNBC), CheckMate 7FL (nivolumab in early-stage TNBC)
Validated.Unnamed: 54: Nivolumab + ipilimumab (CheckMate 8HW and CheckMate 142 trials), dostarlimab trials (e.g., NCT06646445), and other PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor trials in MSI-H/dMMR CRC
Validated.Unnamed: 55: POLARIX trial (polatuzumab vedotin + R-CHP vs R-CHOP, NCT03274492), waveLINE-011 trial (zilovertamab vedotin + R-CHP vs polatuzumab vedotin + R-CHP, NCT06890884), ongoing trials of bispecific antibodies including glofitamab and epcoritamab for DLBCL, other investigational ADC combinations in development
Validated.Unnamed: 56: Osimertinib (FLAURA trial, NCT02296125), amivantamab + lazertinib (MARIPOSA trial, NCT04487080), MARIPOSA-2 (NCT04988295, post-osimertinib setting), ivonescimab + chemotherapy (NCT06396065), patritumab deruxtecan (HERTHENA-Lung02), sacituzumab tirumotecan (OptiTROP-Lung04), izalontamab brengitecan (BL-B01D1-101, BL-B01D1-203), JIN-A02 (Phase 1/2)
Validated.Unnamed: 57: EV-302 (enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab for advanced urothelial cancer), KEYNOTE-905/EV-303 (pembrolizumab-based perioperative therapy), VOLGA (durvalumab + tremelimumab + enfortumab vedotin, NCT04960709), CheckMate 274 (nivolumab adjuvant therapy), AMBASSADOR (pembrolizumab adjuvant therapy, NCT03244384)
Validated.Unnamed: 58: Pembrolizumab (KEYNOTE-522 for neoadjuvant TNBC); sacituzumab govitecan (ASCENT-03 for first-line advanced TNBC); atezolizumab (IMpassion130 for metastatic TNBC); durvalumab (GeparNuevo for early TNBC); olaparib/talazoparib (for BRCA-mutated TNBC); multiple cancer vaccine trials in development; no direct competing adjuvant vaccine trials identified
Validated.Unnamed: 59: Brexucabtagene autoleucel (approved CAR-T, NCT02601313), pirtobrutinib (BTK inhibitor, NCT04662255), venetoclax combinations in various trials, acalabrutinib + obinutuzumab + glofitamab (NCT06054776)
Validated.Unnamed: 60: NCT02010606 (DCVax-L dendritic cell vaccine), NCT02209948 (phase II comparison of 6 vs 12 cycles of temozolomide), NCT05095376 (Phase III trial of lomustine-temozolomide combination therapy vs standard temozolomide in newly diagnosed GBM with MGMT promoter methylation), and various trials evaluating dose-dense temozolomide regimens and immunotherapies.
Validated.Unnamed: 61: Major competing trials include monarchE (abemaciclib), NATALEE (ribociclib), PENELOPE-B, PALLAS, and ongoing studies evaluating novel SERDs (such as elacestrant, camizestrant, and imlunestrant) in combination with CDK4/6 inhibitors. Notable competing therapies are palbociclib and ribociclib in both metastatic and adjuvant settings.
Validated.Unnamed: 62: OlympiAD (talazoparib in metastatic setting), EMBRACA (talazoparib Phase 3 in metastatic BRCA+ breast cancer)
Validated.Unnamed: 63: Key competing drugs include sotorasib (Lumakras), adagrasib (Krazati), and elironrasib. Notable ongoing trials include CodeBreaK 200 (NCT04185883) for sotorasib, KRYSTAL-12 (NCT04685135) for adagrasib, and Phase I/II trials for elironrasib in post-KRAS G12C inhibitor patients. Additional trials include FMC-376 (NCT07020221), BBO-11818, and BBO-8520 for KRAS G12C-mutated solid tumors.
Validated.Unnamed: 64: TROPION-Lung01 (datopotamab deruxtecan, NCT05215340), MARIPOSA-2 (amivantamab + chemotherapy, NCT04988295), OptiTROP-Lung03 (sacituzumab tirumotecan vs docetaxel, NCT05631262), and other Phase 3 trials evaluating TROP2 ADCs and EGFR-targeted therapies in NSCLC
Validated.Unnamed: 65: Pembrolizumab (NICHE trial and other neoadjuvant studies in dMMR rectal cancer), nivolumab (various neoadjuvant immunotherapy trials), ipilimumab (combination trials with nivolumab for dMMR/MSI-H colorectal cancer), and other PD-1 inhibitors in development for organ-sparing approaches in dMMR/MSI-H rectal cancer
Validated.Unnamed: 66: Tarlatamab (DeLLphi-301, NCT05060016, approved May 2024), lurbinectedin + atezolizumab (IMforte, NCT05091567), datopotamab deruxtecan in SCLC trials, other B7-H3 and DLL3-targeted therapies in development
Validated.Unnamed: 67: Key competing trials in relapsed ES-SCLC include trials of tarlatamab (DLL3 bispecific, now approved), ifinatamab deruxtecan (I-DXd, DLL3 ADC in IDEATE-LUNG01), and other DLL3-targeting bispecifics. Lurbinectedin is already approved as a competing option. Additional notable trials include those for HPN328 and BI 764532 (DLL3 bispecifics) and OBI-902 (TROP2 ADC).
Validated.Unnamed: 68: DESTINY-Lung02 (trastuzumab deruxtecan), DESTINY-Lung04 (trastuzumab deruxtecan), and other HER2-targeting trials in development
Validated.Unnamed: 69: Osimertinib monotherapy (standard of care), amivantamab + osimertinib combinations (MARIPOSA trials), osimertinib + chemotherapy combinations (FLAURA2 trial), other EGFR TKI + chemotherapy combinations, EGFR x HER3 ADC trials (e.g., patritumab deruxtecan, AVZO-1418/DB-1418), and TROP2 ADC trials (e.g., sacituzumab tirumotecan, OptiTROP-Lung04)
Validated.Unnamed: 70: DESTINY-Breast09 (NCT04784715), EmpowHER-303 (NCT06435429), and other ongoing trials evaluating T-DXd, zanidatamab, trastuzumab rezetecan, zongertinib, and other HER2-targeted therapies in HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer.

Validated.Unnamed: 72:
Key competing drugs: Docetaxel (standard comparator), ipilimumab (approved CTLA-4 inhibitor), other chemotherapy agents

Validated.Unnamed: 73: MARIPOSA trial (amivantamab + lazertinib vs osimertinib, NCT04487080); FLAURA trial (osimertinib monotherapy vs first-generation TKIs); OptiTROP-Lung04 trial (sacituzumab tirumotecan in EGFR TKI-resistant NSCLC); other EGFR-TKI combination trials; and trials evaluating fourth-generation EGFR-TKIs (e.g., JIN-A02)
Validated.Unnamed: 74: Key competing drugs: alectinib (superior efficacy in ALINA trial), brigatinib, lorlatinib. Validated.Unnamed: 70: The EmpowHER-303 trial (NCT06435429) is currently enrolling participants as of November 2025. The study is a global, Phase 3, open-label trial evaluating zanidatamab in combination with physician's choice chemotherapy versus trastuzumab plus chemotherapy in patients with HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer who have progressed on or are intolerant to prior trastuzumab deruxtecan treatment. The trial is being conducted at approximately 170 sites worldwide and aims to enroll about 550 participants.
Validated.Unnamed: 71: Recruitment complete; estimated primary completion October 2025

Validated.Unnamed: 75: Phase 1 trial completed as of November 2024, with 26 patients enrolled across three cohorts. Final results to be presented at the San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium on December 11, 2025. Phase 2 trial in neoadjuvant setting planned to commence in 2025, with IND sponsorship transferring from Cleveland Clinic to Anixa Biosciences.
Validated.Unnamed: 76: Enrollment completed; 2-year primary analysis results published December 2024 in JAMA and presented at SABCS 2024; data lock June 30, 2024; currently in long-term follow-up phase with BCRF support

Validated.Unnamed: 19: Met primary PFS endpoint at pre-specified interim analysis; no further efficacy or safety details disclosed
Validated.Unnamed: 20: Median OS 34 months with triplet vs 27 months with doublet (33% reduction in risk of death)
Validated.Unnamed: 21: Median PFS 11.3 months with ARX788 vs 8.2 months with LC (HR 0.64, p=0.0006)
Validated.Unnamed: 22: ORR 60.9% vs 40.0% (OR 2.443; p=0.0008); PFS significantly improved
Validated.Unnamed: 23: 3-year DFS 86.4% (atezolizumab + mFOLFOX6) vs 76.6% (mFOLFOX6 alone); HR 0.50 (95% CI 0.35-0.72); p<0.0001; 50% reduction in risk of recurrence or death
Validated.Unnamed: 24: Final results (not interim): Group A (PIK3CA exon 9/20): HR 0.49 (95% CI 0.24-0.98; p=0.044); Group B (other PI3K): HR 0.42 (95% CI 0.21-0.83; p=0.013); recurrence reduction >50% in both groups
Validated.Unnamed: 25: Phase 2 IOV-COM-202 cohort 1A: 67% ORR with combination and durable responses
Validated.Unnamed: 26: Phase 1 (NCT03005782): 57% ORR, 25% complete response in 98 patients; safety similar to anti-PD-1 monotherapy. No interim results from the phase 3 Harmony Head to Head trial have been publicly disclosed as of November 2025.
Validated.Unnamed: 27: Median PFS 19.4 months vs 11.0 months (23% risk reduction, p=0.056); narrowly missed significance (p≤0.045); PD-L1- subgroup HR 0.54
Validated.Unnamed: 28: Final primary endpoint results presented at ASCO 2025: rPFS HR 0.63 (95% CI 0.49-0.80, p=0.0001) for HRR+ population; HR 0.52 (95% CI 0.37-0.72, p<0.0001) for BRCA+ subgroup. OS data immature (HR 0.79, not significant).
Validated.Unnamed: 29: Median DFS not reached vs 86.1 months (HR 0.70; 95% CI 0.52-0.94; p=0.0155); 30% risk reduction; PC-specific DFS 38% risk reduction
Validated.Unnamed: 31: Primary analysis showed significant PFS benefit (HR 0.54, 95% CI 0.43-0.67, p<0.0001) with median PFS 5.4 months vs 2.1 months; OS benefit demonstrated with median OS 13.2 months vs 10.6 months (HR 0.73, 95% CI 0.58-0.92, p=0.0068)
Validated.Unnamed: 33: Significant improvement in progression-free survival (median 10.1 vs 5.4 months in HR+ cohort, HR 0.51) and overall survival (median 23.9 vs 17.5 months, HR 0.64) compared to chemotherapy
Validated.Unnamed: 34: Final analysis showed significant PFS improvement (median 15.0 vs 7.3 months, HR 0.43, p<0.001) and OS benefit (median 34.0 vs 27.0 months, HR 0.67, p=0.019); ORR 58.4% vs 25.0%
Validated.Unnamed: 35: Preplanned interim analysis (announced August 2025) showed ORR 89% vs 74% (p<0.0001) and PFS HR 0.21 (95% CI: 0.13-0.33, p<0.0001), representing 79% reduction in risk of disease progression or death. Median PFS not reached in epcoritamab arm vs 11.2 months in control arm.
Validated.Unnamed: 36: Median PFS 23.9 months vs 14.1 months with everolimus (p=0.022); ORR 21.9% vs 4.2% (p<0.0001); median OS 63.4 months vs 58.7 months (not statistically significant)
Validated.Unnamed: 37: Interim analysis (announced August 2025) showed statistically significant improvement in event-free survival (primary endpoint), as well as improvements in overall survival and pathologic complete response rate; no new safety signals identified
Validated.Unnamed: 38: Prespecified interim analysis (data cutoff January 29, 2025) showed significant OS benefit: median OS 13.6 months vs 8.3 months with chemotherapy (HR 0.60, P<0.001); median PFS 4.2 months vs 3.2 months (HR 0.72, P<0.001); ORR 35% vs 20%
Validated.Unnamed: 39: No interim results publicly disclosed for this trial
Validated.Unnamed: 40: Interim analysis showed statistically significant and clinically meaningful improvement in invasive disease-free survival versus standard-of-care endocrine therapy (specific HR and p-value not disclosed); overall survival data immature but showing positive trend
Validated.Unnamed: 41: Interim analysis showed significant PFS benefit: Pancreatic NETs: median PFS 13.8 months vs 4.4 months placebo; Extra-pancreatic NETs: 8.4 months vs 3.9 months. The study met its primary endpoint and was stopped early for efficacy.
Validated.Unnamed: 42: Interim analysis showed median PFS of 15.0 months with inavolisib + palbociclib + fulvestrant vs 7.3 months with placebo + palbociclib + fulvestrant; more than doubled progression-free survival
Validated.Unnamed: 44: Topline results announced July 2025: Primary endpoint met with pirtobrutinib demonstrating ORR noninferiority vs ibrutinib (nominal P < .05 favoring pirtobrutinib in ITT population); PFS data immature but trending favorably for pirtobrutinib in treatment-naïve subset; safety profile consistent with prior trials

Validated.Unnamed: 46: Significant improvement in PFS (HR 0.53, 95% CI 0.407-0.677, p<0.0001; median PFS 12.8 vs 7.1 months) and OS (HR 0.49, 95% CI 0.375-0.632, p<0.0001; median OS 30.3 vs 15.1 months); ORR 60.9% vs 40.0% (p=0.0008)
Validated.Unnamed: 48: No interim results publicly disclosed
Validated.Unnamed: 49: Third interim analysis (6.1 years median follow-up) showed sustained benefit: invasive disease-free survival HR 0.65 (95% CI 0.53-0.78), distant disease-free survival HR 0.65 (95% CI 0.53-0.81), overall survival HR 0.72 (95% CI 0.56-0.93) with 28% reduction in risk of death
Validated.Unnamed: 50: Significant improvement in disease-free survival (HR 0.50; 3-year DFS 86.4% vs 76.6%; p<0.0001)

Validated.Unnamed: 54: Significant improvement in PFS (HR 0.60, 95% CI 0.45-0.80, P=0.0002); OS data immature at interim analysis
Validated.Unnamed: 56: Primary PFS analysis (June 2024) showed significant benefit: median PFS 23.7 vs 16.6 months (HR 0.70, P<0.001); OS data immature at that time
Validated.Unnamed: 57: Positive interim analysis (2024): Median EFS not reached in durvalumab arm vs 46.1 months in chemotherapy arm (HR 0.68, 95% CI 0.56-0.82, p<0.0001); Median OS not reached in either arm (HR 0.75, 95% CI 0.59-0.93, p=0.0106); pCR rate 33.8% vs 25.8% (OR 1.49, p=0.0038)
Validated.Unnamed: 58: Phase 1 results presented at SITC 2024: vaccine demonstrated safety and tolerability across all three cohorts; strong immune responses observed in Cohort 1a; similar safety profile in Cohort 1b with ongoing analysis; no major adverse events in Cohort 1c (vaccine + pembrolizumab) with confirmed immune responses
Validated.Unnamed: 60: No interim results publicly disclosed. Trial was suspended before completion.
Validated.Unnamed: 61: Interim analysis showed a 38% reduction in progression or death with imlunestrant monotherapy versus standard endocrine therapy in patients with ESR1 mutations, and a 43% reduction with imlunestrant plus abemaciclib versus imlunestrant monotherapy in all patients regardless of ESR1 mutation status. Validated.Unnamed: 63: Phase 1 results published April 2025: ORR 73.5% (25/34) in G12Ci-naive patients; 66.7% in NSCLC, 88.9% in CRC, 75.0% in PDAC. Grade 3 TRAEs in 16.7% of patients; no grade 4/5 TRAEs. D3S-001 600 mg selected for further investigation.
Validated.Unnamed: 64: No interim results publicly disclosed for NCT06132958; however, related Phase 2 trials (OptiTROP-Lung01, NCT05351788 and OptiTROP-Lung03, NCT05631262) showed promising efficacy with ORR of 45-78% and manageable safety profile
Validated.Unnamed: 65: No interim results from AZUR-1 reported as of November 2025. Trial initiated based on predecessor phase 2 study (NCT04165772) showing 100% clinical complete response in 42 patients with median follow-up of 26.3 months.

Validated.Unnamed: 69: None reported for Phase 3 trial; Phase 2 data (NCT05880706) showed 100% ORR, 95% confirmed ORR, 92.1% 12-month PFS rate

Validated.Unnamed: 74: Final analysis results disclosed (trial terminated April 2025): Adjuvant crizotinib did not improve DFS (HR not significantly different from 1.0) or OS compared to observation. Grade 3+ treatment-related adverse events occurred in 43% of crizotinib patients. Treatment discontinuation due to adverse effects occurred in 27% of patients.
Validated.Unnamed: 75: Safe and well-tolerated across all three cohorts. Primary adverse event was injection site irritation; no flu-like symptoms, abnormal laboratory tests, or major systemic adverse events observed, including in combination with pembrolizumab. Over 70% of patients exhibited protocol-defined immune responses with α-lactalbumin-specific T cell induced interferon gamma and interleukin-17. Maximum tolerated dose successfully identified.
Validated.Unnamed: 76: 2-year primary analysis results published December 2024: 2-year ipsilateral invasive cancer rate was 5.9% (95% CI, 3.71-8.04) in guideline-concordant care arm vs 4.2% (95% CI, 2.31-6.00) in active monitoring arm (difference 1.7%), demonstrating noninferiority. No significant differences in invasive tumor size, node status, or tumor grade between groups. Quality of life remained stable and similar between arms.

Validated.Unnamed: 5: Most common grade 3+ AEs: neutropenia (43%), diarrhea (9%), leukopenia (7%); safety profile consistent with prior studies; no new safety signals or protocol modifications
Validated.Unnamed: 6: No new safety signals reported; safety profile consistent with known toxicities of sacituzumab govitecan and pembrolizumab
Validated.Unnamed: 7: Mostly grade 1-2 rash, diarrhea; no protocol modifications reported
Validated.Unnamed: 13: None reported; well-tolerated in Phase 2
Validated.Unnamed: 14: Manageable safety profile in Phase 1/2 SOHO-01, with diarrhea being the most common adverse event. No significant safety signals or protocol modifications reported
Validated.Unnamed: 15: No significant safety signals or protocol modifications reported for this trial; previous ATR inhibitor trials in NSCLC were unsuccessful
