Hold lifted December 2024 with protocol modified to enroll only squamous NSCLC patients. Gotistobart designed to have fewer immune-related adverse effects than traditional CTLA-4 inhibitors.
Validated.Unnamed: 17: No new or alarming safety concerns reported; safety monitoring focused on related and unexpected grade 3 or 4 adverse events and all grade 5 events
Validated.Unnamed: 18: Interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis observed in ~10% of T-DXd patients (predominantly grade 1-2); manageable safety profile with appropriate monitoring; no new safety signals identified
Validated.Unnamed: 20: Similar toxicity to individual drugs; 6.8% discontinued due to AEs. Key AEs: hyperglycemia, stomatitis, diarrhea, rash (manageable with dose modifications)
Validated.Unnamed: 21: Interstitial lung disease (ILD) and ocular toxicity were the main safety signals. ILD occurred in 32.3% of patients (mostly grade 1-2), with 3 possible drug-related deaths. Ocular events occurred in 74.5% (mostly grade 1-2). No protocol modifications reported.
Validated.Unnamed: 22: No new safety signals
Validated.Unnamed: 23: Treatment-related grade ≥3 AEs: 71.7% (atezolizumab + mFOLFOX6) vs 62.1% (mFOLFOX6 alone); manageable safety profile with increase in nonfebrile neutropenia; no significant differences in immune-related AEs
Validated.Unnamed: 24: Three aspirin-related severe adverse events reported: one GI bleeding, one hematoma, one allergic reaction. Overall severe adverse events were more frequent with aspirin (16.8%) vs placebo (11.6%)
Validated.Unnamed: 26: Safety similar to single-agent anti-PD-1; no significant safety signals or protocol modifications reported for the phase 3 Harmony Head to Head trial as of November 2025.
Validated.Unnamed: 27: No new safety signals beyond pembrolizumab; well-tolerated
Validated.Unnamed: 28: Grade 3-4 adverse events: 75% (niraparib arm) vs 59% (placebo arm). Most common: anemia (29%, 25% requiring transfusion), hypertension (27%). Treatment discontinuation due to AEs: 14.7% vs 10.3%. Treatment-emergent deaths: 14 (niraparib) vs 7 (placebo).
Validated.Unnamed: 29: Common AEs: chills (33.4% vs 8.6%), flu-like symptoms (30.5% vs 13.8%), fever (25.1% vs 3.9%); mostly grade 1-2, self-limited
Validated.Unnamed: 31: No new or unexpected safety signals; treatment-related adverse events occurred in 83.5% (combination) vs 40.0% (monotherapy); Grade 3/4 TRAEs in 25.6% vs 5.8%; treatment discontinuation due to AEs in 6.2% vs 3.3%; no protocol modifications disclosed
Validated.Unnamed: 33: Interstitial lung disease (ILD)/pneumonitis observed in 12.1% of T-DXd patients (0.8% grade 5); manageable safety profile overall with no unexpected safety signals
Validated.Unnamed: 34: Manageable safety profile with higher rates of hyperglycemia, stomatitis, and GI toxicity vs placebo; low discontinuation rate (6.8% vs 0.6%); no new safety signals with longer follow-up
Validated.Unnamed: 35: Safety profile consistent with known profiles of individual agents. CRS occurred in 24% of patients (primarily low grade: 19% Grade 1, 5% Grade 2). Single ICANS event (0.8%, Grade 1). Boxed warnings for CRS and ICANS. No new safety signals identified.
Validated.Unnamed: 36: 177Lu-edotreotide was well-tolerated with no unforeseen treatment-emergent adverse events; one grade 2 serious TEAE of myelodysplastic syndrome reported; lower discontinuation rate due to TEAEs (1.8%) vs everolimus (15.2%)
Validated.Unnamed: 37: No new safety signals identified in interim analysis; known toxicities include dermatologic events (enfortumab vedotin-related) and immune-related adverse events (pembrolizumab-related)
Validated.Unnamed: 38: Boxed Warning for life-threatening or fatal cytokine release syndrome and neurologic toxicity, including immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome (ICANS); warnings and precautions for cytopenias, infections, hepatotoxicity, hypersensitivity, and embryo-fetal toxicity
Validated.Unnamed: 40: No unexpected safety findings; adverse events consistent with known safety profile; well tolerated
Validated.Unnamed: 41: No significant safety signals or protocol modifications reported; the safety profile was manageable and consistent with previous cabozantinib studies.
Validated.Unnamed: 42: Grade 3/4 hyperglycemia, stomatitis, diarrhea, and ocular toxic effects (dry eye, blurred vision) reported; no new safety signals or protocol modifications disclosed
Validated.Unnamed: 43: No significant safety signals or protocol modifications reported
Validated.Unnamed: 44: No significant safety signals reported; safety profile consistent with prior pirtobrutinib trials
Validated.Unnamed: 45: No new safety signals reported; adverse events consistent with known safety profiles of individual agents; most common AEs were stomatitis (47%), diarrhea (27%), and anemia (24%); grade 1/2 bradycardia observed in 3.8% of giredestrant arm vs 0.5% in control arm; no cases of photopsia; overall toxicity balanced between arms
Validated.Unnamed: 46: No significant safety signals reported; safety profiles consistent with known profiles of each agent
Validated.Unnamed: 48: No significant safety signals or protocol modifications reported
Validated.Unnamed: 49: No significant safety signals or protocol modifications reported; adverse events consistent with known olaparib safety profile; low incidence of MDS/AML (4 olaparib vs 6 placebo at 6-year follow-up)
Validated.Unnamed: 50: Manageable safety profile; treatment-related grade ≥3 AEs in 71.7% (atezolizumab arm) vs 62.1% (mFOLFOX6 alone); increased nonfebrile neutropenia; no protocol modifications reported
Validated.Unnamed: 51: No new safety signals identified; safety profile consistent with known checkpoint inhibitor toxicities; any-grade TRAEs: 81% (nivo+ipi) vs 71% (nivo alone); most common: pruritus (26%), diarrhea (20%), hypothyroidism (17%); grade 3+ TRAEs: 23% vs 17%; manageable with established protocols
Validated.Unnamed: 53: No major protocol modifications reported; safety profile consistent with known profiles of pembrolizumab and chemotherapy components; most common grade 3+ adverse events were neutropenia (34.5%), decreased neutrophil count (18.6%), anemia (18.0%)
Validated.Unnamed: 54: No significant safety signals; pembrolizumab had fewer treatment-related adverse events than chemotherapy (grade 3-5: 22% vs 66%)
Validated.Unnamed: 56: Prophylactic anticoagulation recommended during first 4 months of treatment to reduce venous thromboembolic event (VTE) risk; no major protocol modifications reported
Validated.Unnamed: 57: No new safety signals; safety profile consistent with known durvalumab and platinum-based chemotherapy adverse events; no increased discontinuation rates or impact on surgery completion
Validated.Unnamed: 58: No major adverse events reported across all three cohorts; vaccine demonstrated favorable safety profile; no dose-limiting toxicities identified; combination with pembrolizumab (Cohort 1c) showed no additional safety concerns
Validated.Unnamed: 60: No significant safety signals reported. Trial suspended due to funding constraints, not safety concerns.
Validated.Unnamed: 61: No significant safety signals reported; the safety profile of imlunestrant was favorable, without the oral SERD-specific safety signals of ocular or cardiac issues. The combination with abemaciclib had a safety profile consistent with known fulvestrant-abemaciclib, with a low discontinuation rate of 6.3%. No protocol modifications were reported.
Validated.Unnamed: 62: Protocol amended in 2015 to expand from triple-negative only to include hormone receptor-positive disease. Safety profile favorable with low incidence of AML/MDS (0.2% olaparib vs 0.3% placebo at 3.5 years); no new safety signals identified
Validated.Unnamed: 63: No dose-limiting toxicities or grade 4/5 treatment-related adverse events. Grade 3 TRAEs in 16.7% of G12Ci-naive patients. Dose modifications in 28.6% of patients due to TRAEs; no discontinuations. D3S-001 600 mg selected as recommended dose based on pharmacokinetics.
Validated.Unnamed: 64: No significant safety signals or protocol modifications reported; Phase 2 data showed manageable safety profile with most common grade ≥3 AEs being neutropenia (30%), anemia (16%), and rash (5-6%); no treatment-related deaths
Validated.Unnamed: 65: No significant safety signals or protocol modifications reported. Predecessor phase 2 study showed favorable safety profile with no grade 3 or higher adverse events.
Validated.Unnamed: 66: None reported for Phase 3 trial
Validated.Unnamed: 69: None reported for Phase 3 trial; Phase 1/2 data showed manageable hematologic toxicities (anemia, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, neutropenia) with low discontinuation rate (13%)
Validated.Unnamed: 70: No significant safety signals or protocol modifications have been reported for the EmpowHER-303 trial as of November 2025.
Validated.Unnamed: 71: No significant safety signals or protocol modifications reported
Validated.Unnamed: 72: Partial clinical hold placed October 2024 due to varying results between squamous and non-squamous NSCLC populations; protocol modified December 2024 to restrict enrollment to squamous NSCLC only. Previous Phase 1/2 studies noted toxicity concerns at higher doses.
Validated.Unnamed: 73: Grade ≥3 adverse events higher with combination (64% vs 27%), primarily hematologic and chemotherapy-related. Most common grade ≥3 AEs: neutropenia (30%), anemia (11%), thrombocytopenia (9%). Safety profile manageable; no new safety signals identified. Discontinuation due to AEs: 12% (osimertinib) and 50% (pemetrexed) in combination arm.
Validated.Unnamed: 74: Significant toxicity observed: 43% of patients experienced grade 3 or higher treatment-related adverse events. Most common grade 3+ events included diarrhea (8%), edema (5%), eye disorders, abdominal pain, elevated ALT, and decreased neutrophil count (3% each). One grade 4 stroke reported. Treatment discontinuation due to adverse effects occurred in 27% of patients.
Validated.Unnamed: 75: Mild irritation at injection site; no serious side effects
Validated.Unnamed: 76: No significant safety signals or protocol modifications reported. Trial design allowed for patient crossover between arms (anticipated in statistical analysis plan).

Validated.Unnamed: 2: Phase 1 TROPION-PanTumor01: Promising activity in heavily pre-treated HR+/HER2- breast cancer patients; manageable safety profile with stomatitis as notable adverse event
Validated.Unnamed: 3: SERENA-2 showed PFS benefit vs fulvestrant
Validated.Unnamed: 4: Phase 2 (SERENA-2) demonstrated that camizestrant significantly improved PFS versus fulvestrant in ER+/HER2- advanced breast cancer, supporting Phase 3 evaluation in first-line setting
Validated.Unnamed: 5: Phase 3 ASCENT trial (NCT02574455) in 2L+ mTNBC showed significant OS benefit: median OS 12.1 vs 6.7 months (HR 0.48, P<0.001), leading to FDA approval and supporting evaluation in 1st-line setting
Validated.Unnamed: 6: Strong preclinical synergy; ASCENT 2L validated SG
Validated.Unnamed: 7: Phase 1: At 300 mg dose, median PFS 8.8 months (KRAS G12X) and 8.5 months (broader RAS-mutant); ORR 36% (KRAS G12X) and 27% (RAS-mutant); DCR 91-95%; manageable safety profile
Validated.Unnamed: 8: Phase 1 trial (NCT05379985) in RAS-mutant NSCLC: ORR 38% in RAS G12X-mutant patients (n=40) at 120-220 mg daily; median DOR 15.1 months; manageable safety profile (ELCC 2025)
Validated.Unnamed: 9: Phase 2 ARC-7 study showed encouraging efficacy in PD-L1-high NSCLC with domvanalimab + zimberelimab combination; Phase 3 ARC-10 study was discontinued to focus resources on STAR-121
Validated.Unnamed: 10: Phase I/II ARTEMIDE-01 in NSCLC showed manageable safety profile with early activity (ORR with confirmed partial responses, 6-month DCR ~31% in pretreated patients)
Validated.Unnamed: 11: Phase 1/2 data in NSCLC showed rilvegostomig was active and well tolerated; rationale for biliary tract cancer based on high recurrence rates post-resection
Validated.Unnamed: 13: Phase 2 (KOMET-001): CR/CRh rate 23% (95% CI 15-33%) in R/R NPM1-mutant AML, significantly higher than historical standard-of-care rate of 12%; ORR 33%; median OS 6.6 months; favorable safety profile with manageable adverse events
Validated.Unnamed: 14: Phase 1/2 SOHO-01: ORR 64% in pretreated patients, 71% in treatment-naive patients; manageable safety profile (most recent data from ESMO 2025/ASCO 2025)
Validated.Unnamed: 15: Phase 2 HUDSON study showed promising efficacy: median PFS 6.0 months (80% CI, 4.6-7.5) and median OS 15.9 months (80% CI, 14.1-20.3) in NSCLC patients who progressed on anti-PD-(L)1 and platinum therapy, supporting Phase 3 development
Validated.Unnamed: 16: Phase 1/2 results showed activity in PD-L1-low or "cold" tumors with a differentiated safety profile, including low rates of medically significant immune-related adverse effects and no life-threatening events, supporting the initiation of Phase 3.
Validated.Unnamed: 17: Phase 2 (S1800A): 136 patients showed extended survival vs standard treatment
Validated.Unnamed: 18: DESTINY-Breast04 (Phase 3) demonstrated significant PFS and OS improvement with T-DXd vs chemotherapy in pretreated HER2-low metastatic breast cancer (median PFS 10.1 vs 5.4 months, HR 0.50), establishing proof-of-concept for HER2-targeted therapy in HER2-low disease and supporting earlier-line evaluation in DESTINY-Breast06
Validated.Unnamed: 19: Phase 1/2 trial (NCT05150691) in advanced solid tumors including endometrial cancer demonstrated antitumor activity in HER2-positive and HER2-low expressing tumors with manageable safety profile, leading to FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation in 2023 and initiation of multiple Phase 3 trials
Validated.Unnamed: 20: Phase 1b/2 data showed promising antitumor activity and manageable safety profile with the triplet combination, supporting Phase 3 initiation. Inavolisib demonstrated synergistic activity with palbociclib-fulvestrant in preclinical models
Validated.Unnamed: 21: Phase 1 trial in China: At recommended Phase 2 dose (1.5 mg/kg Q3W), ORR 65.5% (95% CI 45.7-82.1%), DCR 100%, median PFS 17.02 months in heavily pretreated HER2+ metastatic breast cancer. Manageable safety profile with no dose-limiting toxicities.
Validated.Unnamed: 22: Prior approval for 2nd-line BRAF V600E mCRC; historically limited 1st-line efficacy for this mutation
Validated.Unnamed: 23: Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) have shown benefit in metastatic dMMR colorectal cancer, but their efficacy in the adjuvant setting for stage III dMMR/MSI-high colon cancer was uncertain prior to the ATOMIC trial.
Validated.Unnamed: 24: Rationale based on observational studies suggesting aspirin improves disease-free survival in PIK3CA-mutated colorectal cancer; aspirin is a well-established drug with known safety profile
Validated.Unnamed: 25: Phase 2: 67% ORR with lifileucel + pembrolizumab; durable responses in ~20 patients treated
Validated.Unnamed: 27: Phase 1/2: Median progression-free survival (PFS) of 25.5 months with Cylembio plus pembrolizumab; no added systemic toxicity compared to anti-PD-1 monotherapy; encouraging safety and efficacy profile supporting Phase 3 initiation
Validated.Unnamed: 28: MAGNITUDE trial (Phase 3 in mCRPC): Niraparib + AAP showed significant rPFS improvement in BRCA-mutated patients (HR 0.53, 95% CI 0.36-0.79, p=0.0014), representing 47% risk reduction vs AAP alone. Safety profile manageable with increased hematological toxicities. These results supported evaluation in earlier disease stage (mCSPC) in AMPLITUDE.
Validated.Unnamed: 29: Early phase studies demonstrated favorable safety and tolerability of intraprostatic CAN-2409 injections combined with valacyclovir. Phase 1/2 data showed immunogenic responses and preliminary efficacy signals that supported advancement to phase 3 testing in intermediate-to-high-risk localized prostate cancer receiving radiation therapy.
Validated.Unnamed: 30: ARAMIS trial (nmCRPC): Darolutamide significantly improved metastasis-free survival (mFS) and overall survival (OS); ARASENS trial (mHSPC): Darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel significantly improved OS vs. placebo + ADT + docetaxel
Validated.Unnamed: 31: Phase 1/2 data from 2SMALL study showed promising efficacy (ORR 57.7%) and manageable safety profile for lurbinectedin + atezolizumab in second-line ES-SCLC, supporting Phase 3 IMforte trial initiation for first-line maintenance setting
Validated.Unnamed: 33: Phase 1/2 studies showed promising efficacy in HER2-low metastatic breast cancer with ORR of 37.0-37.5% and median PFS of 6.3-11.1 months in heavily pretreated patients, supporting Phase 3 development
Validated.Unnamed: 34: Phase 1/2 studies demonstrated a manageable safety profile and significant improvement in progression-free survival, supporting the advancement to Phase 3.
Validated.Unnamed: 35: Phase 1/2 showed 82% ORR with 60% CR in heavily pretreated R/R FL, with manageable safety profile and primarily low-grade CRS and ICANS events
Validated.Unnamed: 36: A retrospective Phase 2 study with 177Lu-edotreotide demonstrated positive results, supporting Phase 3 initiation and leading to FDA Fast Track Designation. The Phase 2 data showed favorable efficacy and safety, with no unforeseen treatment-emergent adverse events.
Validated.Unnamed: 37: Phase 1b/2 EV-103 trial showed high response rates (ORR 73.3%, CR 15.6%) and manageable safety with enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab in first-line metastatic urothelial carcinoma; Phase 3 EV-302 trial demonstrated significant survival benefit (HR 0.47 for OS) in first-line metastatic disease, supporting investigation in earlier-stage MIBC
Validated.Unnamed: 38: Phase 1 (DeLLphi-300): ORR 23.4%, median DOR 12.3 months, median OS 13.2 months in heavily pretreated SCLC; manageable safety profile with 31% grade ≥3 TRAEs. Phase 2 (DeLLphi-301): ORR 40% (10 mg dose) with durable responses; led to FDA accelerated approval May 2024.
Validated.Unnamed: 39: Phase 1b/2 EMBER study: Imlunestrant demonstrated manageable safety profile and preliminary antitumor activity in ER+/HER2- advanced breast cancer, with median PFS of 7.2 months at 400 mg dose in heavily pretreated patients (92% prior CDK4/6i exposure). Recommended phase 2 dose established at 400 mg once daily.
Validated.Unnamed: 40: Phase 2 coopERA trial (neoadjuvant setting) showed giredestrant was superior to aromatase inhibitor in reducing malignant cell division (Ki67 levels) in ER+ HER2- early breast cancer, supporting advancement to Phase 3 adjuvant trial
Validated.Unnamed: 41: Phase 1/2 studies demonstrated manageable safety and promising efficacy, supporting the initiation of the phase 3 CABINET trial. Early data showed significant delays in disease progression and favorable safety profile, leading to the decision to advance to phase 3.
Validated.Unnamed: 42: Phase 1b/2 results demonstrated a manageable safety profile and promising efficacy, with median progression-free survival (PFS) of 15.0 months for inavolisib + palbociclib + fulvestrant versus 7.3 months for placebo + palbociclib + fulvestrant, more than doubling PFS. Grade 3/4 hyperglycemia, stomatitis, diarrhea, and ocular toxic effects were reported, but no new safety signals or protocol modifications were disclosed.
Validated.Unnamed: 43: Phase 1/2 LINKER-MM1 trial showed an objective response rate (ORR) of 70% (95% CI: 59, 80) at the 200 mg dose, with 50% achieving a complete response or better. The median duration of response was 29.4 months. Safety profile was manageable, with cytokine release syndrome (CRS) occurring in 46% of patients (mostly Grade 1-2) and neurologic toxicity including ICANS in 54% (Grade 3 or 4 in 8%).
Validated.Unnamed: 44: Phase 1/2 BRUIN trial in CLL/SLL showed an overall response rate (ORR) of 73% in 317 patients, with a median duration of response of 16.6 months and manageable safety profile
Validated.Unnamed: 45: Phase 1/2 data demonstrated acceptable safety profile and preliminary efficacy signals supporting advancement to Phase 3; specific Phase 2 results not publicly detailed in available sources
Validated.Unnamed: 46: The BEACON CRC trial demonstrated significant benefit in the later-line setting, with improved response rates and survival in patients with BRAF V600E-mutated metastatic colorectal cancer. The combination of encorafenib and cetuximab showed superior efficacy compared to standard chemotherapy, supporting the rationale for the Phase 3 BREAKWATER trial in the first-line setting.
Validated.Unnamed: 47: Phase 1/2 trials in recurrent H3K27M-mutant diffuse glioma demonstrated 20% overall response rate (ORR) by RANO-HGG criteria (95% CI, 10.0-33.7%), with median duration of response of 11.2 months (95% CI, 3.8-not reached). The agent was well-tolerated with fatigue as the most common adverse event (10% grade 3+). These durable responses in heavily pretreated patients supported advancement to Phase 3 in newly diagnosed disease.
Validated.Unnamed: 48: Phase 2 ELM-2 showed 52% ORR and 31.5% CR in heavily pretreated R/R DLBCL
Validated.Unnamed: 49: Phase 2 studies demonstrated PARP inhibitor efficacy in BRCA-mutant cancers with manageable safety profile; olaparib showed activity in metastatic BRCA-mutant breast cancer (OlympiAD trial), supporting evaluation in adjuvant setting
Validated.Unnamed: 50: Phase 2 data supported efficacy in dMMR/MSI-H colorectal cancer; immune checkpoint inhibitors showed benefit in metastatic dMMR setting, supporting adjuvant evaluation
Validated.Unnamed: 51: CheckMate-142 (Phase 2) demonstrated durable responses and manageable safety with nivolumab + ipilimumab in previously treated MSI-H/dMMR mCRC, with ORR of 55% and 12-month PFS of 71%, supporting initiation of CheckMate-8HW for first-line evaluation
Validated.Unnamed: 52: Phase 1 data showed 57% ORR in treatment-naive patients and 46% ORR in those previously treated with anti-PD-1, with 25-31% complete response rates and an acceptable safety profile.
Validated.Unnamed: 53: Phase 2 showed significant improvement in pathologic complete response
Validated.Unnamed: 54: Phase 2 KEYNOTE-016 and other early studies demonstrated durable responses and high objective response rates with pembrolizumab in previously treated MSI-H/dMMR solid tumors, including colorectal cancer, supporting the rationale for Phase 3 development in the first-line setting
Validated.Unnamed: 55: Phase 2 waveLINE-007 (NCT05406401) showed a 100% complete response rate at the recommended Phase 2 dose of 1.75 mg/kg zilovertamab vedotin plus R-CHP in previously untreated DLBCL, with manageable safety profile (presented at ASH 2024)
Validated.Unnamed: 56: Phase 1b CHRYSALIS study: Amivantamab + lazertinib demonstrated antitumor activity in treatment-naive EGFR+ NSCLC with manageable safety profile, supporting Phase 3 advancement
Validated.Unnamed: 57: ABACUS (Phase 2) showed benefit of neoadjuvant durvalumab monotherapy in MIBC, with manageable safety profile, supporting Phase 3 NIAGARA trial design combining durvalumab with chemotherapy
Validated.Unnamed: 58: Phase 1 results show the vaccine is well tolerated, with more than 70% of participants demonstrating protocol-defined immune responses. No major adverse events or dose-limiting toxicities were reported across all cohorts, including those receiving the vaccine in combination with pembrolizumab. These findings support the advancement to Phase 2 trials.
Validated.Unnamed: 59: Phase 1/2 (NCT05471843) met primary endpoint of ORR in heavily pretreated R/R MCL patients post-BTK inhibitor and anti-CD20 therapy, showing high response rates including complete responses, with manageable safety profile and no dose-limiting toxicities; results supported FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation in October 2025
Validated.Unnamed: 60: Phase 1 data showed 55% three-year overall survival (vs 16% historical SOC), 38% disease-free at 3 years (vs 6% historical), median OS 38.4 months (vs 14.6 months historical), median PFS 17 months (vs 6.9 months historical), and 12-month disease-free survival 75% (vs 26.9% historical). No serious adverse events were reported, with minor side effects limited to fatigue, skin rash, and pruritis. Phase 2 randomized trial (124 patients) showed PFS improvement of 2.2 months (p=0.011) though OS primary endpoint was not met; vaccine was well tolerated with maintained quality of life.
Validated.Unnamed: 61: Phase 2: ORR 45%, median PFS 8.2 months in pretreated NSCLC (published Lancet Oncology 2024); Phase 1b/2: Manageable safety profile, ORR 38% in SCLC (ASCO 2023); Phase 2: Superior PFS vs. chemotherapy (HR 0.55) in biomarker-selected population
Validated.Unnamed: 62: Phase 2 metastatic breast cancer (OlympiAD): Olaparib showed significant PFS benefit vs chemotherapy in BRCA-mutated metastatic breast cancer (median PFS 7.0 vs 4.2 months, HR 0.58), with manageable safety profile, supporting Phase 3 adjuvant study
Validated.Unnamed: 63: Phase 1 results (published April 2025): ORR 73.5% (25/34) in G12Ci-naive patients overall; 66.7% in NSCLC (14/21), 88.9% in CRC (8/9), 75.0% in PDAC (3/4). Favorable safety profile with no grade 4/5 treatment-related adverse events. Grade 3 TRAEs in 16.7% of patients. D3S-001 600 mg selected for Phase 2 expansion based on pharmacokinetics and efficacy.
Validated.Unnamed: 64: Phase 1/2 data (OptiTROP-Lung01, NCT05351788) in EGFR-mutated NSCLC showed ORR of 48.6-77.6%, DCR of 94.6-100%, and median PFS of 15.4 months with manageable safety profile; Phase 2 data (OptiTROP-Lung03, NCT05631262) showed ORR of 45.1% vs 15.6% with docetaxel, supporting Phase 3 advancement
Validated.Unnamed: 65: Phase 2 (NCT04165772): 100% clinical complete response (cCR) in all 42 patients who completed 6 months of dostarlimab treatment; sustained cCR with median follow-up of 26.3 months in first 24 patients; no grade 3 or higher adverse events reported; results published in NEJM 2022 and led to FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation
Validated.Unnamed: 66: Phase 2 IDeate-Lung01 (NCT05280470): ORR 48.2% (95% CI 39.6-56.9%) at 12 mg/kg dose, median DOR 5.3 months, DCR 87.6%, median TTR 1.4 months (presented at WCLC September 2025). Phase 1/2 IDeate-PanTumor01 (NCT04145622): ORR 52.4%, median DOR 5.9 months, median OS 12.2 months in 21 ES-SCLC patients. Manageable safety profile supported Phase 3 advancement.
Validated.Unnamed: 67: Phase 2 (TROPiCS-03): ORR 41.9% (95% CI, 27.0%-57.9%), median PFS 4.4 months, median OS 13.6 months in 2nd-line ES-SCLC; manageable safety profile consistent with prior studies (IASLC 2024)
Validated.Unnamed: 68: Phase I/II SOHO-01 study demonstrated promising safety and efficacy as a second-line therapy in patients with advanced NSCLC and HER2 mutations, supporting progression to Phase III
Validated.Unnamed: 69: Phase 2 combination data (iza-bren + osimertinib): 100% ORR, 95% confirmed ORR, 92.1% 12-month PFS rate, manageable hematologic toxicities (NCT05880706, presented WCLC 2025)
Validated.Unnamed: 70: Phase 2: In the DESTINY-Breast03 trial, trastuzumab deruxtecan demonstrated a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 25.1 months versus 5.4 months with trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1), with an objective response rate (ORR) of 79.7% versus 34.2%. The safety profile was manageable, with the most common grade 3 or higher adverse events being neutropenia (12%) and interstitial lung disease (ILD) (10%). These results led to the initiation of Phase 3 trials for further evaluation in earlier lines of therapy.
Validated.Unnamed: 71: Phase 2 HUDSON study: ORR 13.9%, median PFS 6.0 months, median OS 15.9 months in immunotherapy-refractory NSCLC; manageable safety profile supported Phase 3 advancement
Validated.Unnamed: 72: Phase 1/2 results showed promising efficacy and safety, supporting dose selection for Phase 3. The Stage I part of PRESERVE-003 and earlier trials demonstrated activity in squamous NSCLC, with the high dose selected for Phase 3 based on safety, efficacy, and exposure-response analyses. Toxicity was manageable, and the mechanism supports potential in low TPS and STK-11/KEAP-1 mutated tumors, but efficacy and safety signals were stronger in squamous NSCLC.
Validated.Unnamed: 73: Phase 2 OPAL trial (NEJ032C/LOGIK1801) evaluated osimertinib plus platinum-pemetrexed in previously untreated EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC. Results showed excellent efficacy with acceptable toxicity; median PFS 31.0 months (95% CI not reported) with ORR as primary endpoint. Safety profile was manageable with grade ≥3 AEs in approximately 42-47% of patients. These results supported the design of the Phase 3 FLAURA2 trial.
Validated.Unnamed: 74: Phase 1/2 results demonstrated high response rates (approximately 60% ORR) and manageable safety profile in ALK-positive NSCLC, with progression-free survival of 7-10 months in metastatic setting, supporting progression to Phase 3 adjuvant trial.
Validated.Unnamed: 75: Phase 1 clinical trial results show the vaccine is well tolerated, with more than 70% of patients exhibiting protocol-defined immune responses. The primary adverse event was mild injection site irritation, with no serious side effects observed. The vaccine induced α-lactalbumin-specific T cell responses with interferon gamma and interleukin-17 production, supporting its advancement to Phase 2.
Validated.Unnamed: 76: N/A - Phase 3 trial designed based on clinical rationale and observational data about DCIS overtreatment, not based on previous phase drug development results
